title: Capture Reward Claims
excerpt: Physical KOTH and Outpost reward chests are intentionally deferred until they can be implemented as one shared service.
bodyMarkdown:
# Capture Reward Claims

Physical KOTH and Outpost reward chests are intentionally deferred until they can be implemented as one shared service.

The required service should be named along these lines:

- `CaptureRewardClaimService`
- `CaptureRewardClaim`
- `CaptureRewardSource`
- `CaptureRewardPlacement`
- `CaptureRewardState`

Required behavior before enabling this in production:

- Persist claims in `plugins/Factions/data/capture_reward_claims.json`.
- Store source type, source id, event/session id, winner faction id, reward profile, location, expiration, claimed state, and previous block data.
- Place and remove blocks only on the server thread.
- Never use a normal Bukkit chest inventory as the reward container unless duplication and inventory persistence are intentionally handled.
- Restore the previous block when a claim chest is removed.
- Execute configured reward definitions exactly once.
- Reject enemies, disqualified factions, expired claims, disbanded winners when configured, and repeat claims.
- Save on autosave, `/f saveall`, reload, and shutdown.

Current beta behavior:

- KOTH rewards are still executed by the existing KOTH reward profile path.
- Outpost interval rewards are still executed by the existing outpost reward path.
- The capture state fixes in this pass make reward claim creation safer to add next because abandonment/resume/control changes are now explicit.

title: Practical Command Guide
excerpt: This page explains normal command workflows. The Complete Command Reference contains every syntax, permission and specialized subcommand.
bodyMarkdown:
# Practical Command Guide

This page explains normal command workflows. The Complete Command Reference contains every syntax, permission and specialized subcommand.

## Reading Syntax

- `<value>` is required.
- `[value]` is optional.
- `a|b|c` means choose one value.
- Most commands begin with `/f`; `/factions` is the full root alias.
- Aliases, permission overlays, cooldowns and enabled state can be changed in `commands.yml`.

Use `/f help` for the live permission-filtered index, `/f help <category>` for a category, `/f help <search>` to search, and `/f help text` for text fallback.

## Public Information

| Command | Use |
| --- | --- |
| `/f list` | List factions. |
| `/f show [faction]` | Show faction identity, members, land, power/DTR and configured fields. |
| `/f status` | Show your faction member status. |
| `/f top [page]` | Open the cached FTop leaderboard. |
| `/f top <criteria> [page]` | Sort by a supported FactionsUUID criterion. |
| `/f points [faction]` | Show Season Points. |
| `/f pointstop [page]` | Show the Season Points leaderboard. |
| `/f season` | Open the current season overview. |
| `/f hof` | Show archived Hall of Fame records. |

## Create, Join, and Leave

```text
/f create <name>
/f desc <description...>
/f invite <player>
/f showinvites
/f join <faction>
/f leave
```

Leaders can use `/f open` to control invitation requirements when enabled. `/f deinvite <player>` removes a pending invitation. `/f disband` permanently removes the faction and should require the configured confirmation/role safeguards.

## Members and Roles

```text
/f kick <player>
/f ban <player>
/f unban <player>
/f banlist
/f promote <player>
/f demote <player>
/f coleader <player>
/f defaultrank <role>
/f title <player> [title]
```

The Bukkit node allows the command; faction role/action permissions decide whether the actor may use it inside that faction.

## Roster and Alts

```text
/f roster
/f roster list
/f roster add <player>
/f roster remove <player>
/f roster rank <player> <rank>

/f alt list
/f alt invite <player>
/f alt invites
/f alt join <faction>
/f alt kick <player>
/f alt revoke <player>
/f alt open
/f alt close
/f alt teleport <player>
```

Roster-required joins and alt limits are server-configured. An invitation does not bypass bans or configured maximums.

## Claims and Territory

```text
/f claim [radius] [faction]
/f unclaim [radius] [faction]
/f autoclaim [faction]
/f autounclaim [faction]
/f claimline [amount] [direction] [faction]
/f claimfill [amount] [faction]
/f unclaimfill [amount] [faction]
/f unclaimall
/f map [on|off]
/f seechunk
/f listclaims [world] [faction]
```

`/f claimat <world> <x> <z>` uses **chunk coordinates**, not block coordinates. Large/radius/admin claims require separate permissions.

## Home, Warps, and Travel

```text
/f sethome
/f delhome
/f home
/f setwarp <name> [password]
/f delwarp <name>
/f warp [name] [password]
/f warp menu
/f warp icon <warp>
/f fly
/f stuck
```

Warmups can cancel on movement, damage or combat. Passwords and faction action permissions still apply when using the GUI.

## Internal Permissions and Settings

```text
/f perms
/f perms menu
/f settings
/f settings menu
```

Faction leaders manage their own role/action access. Server-wide relation/global overrides remain staff-controlled.

## Relations and Chat

```text
/f ally <faction>
/f truce <faction>
/f neutral <faction>
/f enemy <faction>
/f chat <public|faction|alliance|truce>
/f togglealliancechat
/f announce <message...>
/f coords
```

Relation wishes may need acceptance. Chat spy is staff-only.

## Faction Money

```text
/f money balance [faction]
/f money deposit <amount> [faction]
/f money withdraw <amount> [faction]
/f money ff <amount> <fromFaction> <toFaction>
/f money fp <amount> <fromFaction> <toPlayer>
/f money pf <amount> <fromPlayer> <toFaction>
```

Money operations require Vault and an economy provider. Permission and faction economy access are checked separately.

## TNT

```text
/f tnt
/f tnt info
/f tnt deposit <amount|all>
/f tnt withdraw <amount|all>
/f tnt fill <radius> <amount>
/f tnt siphon <radius> [amount]
```

`amount` on fill is the maximum total transfer, not the amount per dispenser. Radius, scan budget, valid container types, territory, timers and faction balance are configured in `mxf/tnt.yml`.

## Vaults and Chest

```text
/f chest
/f chest menu
/f vault [number|menu|manager]
/f vaults
```

These are native faction-owned inventories. Manager/admin views require additional permissions.

## Missions, Upgrades, and Shops

```text
/f missions
/f missions info <id>
/f upgrades
/f upgrade info <id>
/f upgrade buy <id>
/f shop
/f shop list [shop] [page]
/f shop buy [shop] <item> confirm
/f shop history [page]
/f pointshop
/f rewards
```

Definitions control what appears. If a menu is empty, staff should validate the relevant definition files instead of adding fake entries.

## Timers and Shields

Players inspect/manage their faction shield with:

```text
/f shield
/f shield status
/f shield set <startTime> <duration>
```

Staff control global timers and shield overrides:

```text
/f grace status
/f grace start <duration>
/f grace stop
/f sotw status
/f sotw start <duration>
/f sotw stop
/f lockshield <faction>
/f resetshield <faction>
```

Duration values support configured suffixes such as `30m`, `2h`, and `1d` where documented.

## Raids and Defense Alerts

```text
/f raid
/f raid info [faction]
/f raid claim create
/f raid claims
/f raid alerts
/f raid alerts history [page]
/f raid alerts detail <id>
/f raid alerts ack <id>
/f raid alerts ackall
/f raid alerts settings
/f raid history [page]
/f raid recap [raidId]
/f war
```

Staff use `/f raid control list|cancel|cleanup`, alert test/inspect/reload commands, and recap admin permission.

## KOTH and Outposts

```text
/f koth
/f koth list
/f koth status [id]
/f outpost
/f outpost list
/f outpost info <id>
/f outpost status <id>
/f outpost top
/f outpost stats [faction]
/f outpost teleport <id>
/f raidoutpost
/f raidoutpost status [id]
/f raidoutpost history [id] [page]
```

Admin creation/region/start/stop/reset commands are documented in KOTH, Outposts, and Raid Outposts.

## Corners

```text
/f corner
/f corner list
/f corner claim <id>
/f corner teleport <id>
/f corner menu
```

The server can require a faction, minimum role, money, confirmation, owner-only teleport and controlled overwrite.

## Seasons and Season Points

Player commands:

```text
/f season
/f season info
/f season phase
/f season top [page]
/f season history [page]
/f season rewards
/f season rewards preview
/f points [faction]
/f pointstop [page]
/f hof
```

Staff lifecycle commands:

```text
/f season start <id> <display name> confirm
/f season transition <phase> [force confirm] [reason]
/f season snapshot
/f season freeze [status|rebuild]
/f season rewards status|preview|confirm|execute
/f season end confirm
/f season archive confirm
```

Starting, ending and archiving record state. They do not automatically reset factions or worlds.

## Analytics and Intelligence

```text
/f analytics
/f intelligence
/f intelligence <faction>
/f intelligence menu [faction]
/f intelligence guide
```

Viewing another faction requires its additional permission. Intelligence uses cached tracked sources and should be interpreted as readiness guidance.

## Rules, Investigations, and Rollback

Players may use `/f rules`, `/f rules list`, `/f rules history`, and `/f rules sanctions` where permitted.

Staff workflows include:

```text
/f rules warn <faction> <rule> [reason]
/f rules strike <faction> <rule> [reason]
/f rules inspect <caseId>
/f rules note <caseId> <note>
/f rules evidence <caseId> <label> <reference> [staffOnly]
/f rules appeal <caseId> <state>
/f rules penalty <caseId> <type> <amount> [durationSeconds]
/f rules undo <caseId>
/f investigate faction <faction> [page]
/f investigate event <eventId>
/f rollback preview <eventId>
/f rollback confirm <token> <reason>
```

Rollback never mutates data during preview. Tokens are generated by a preview and must be confirmed by the correct staff actor before expiry.

## Web Panel

```text
/f web
/f panel link
/f web sessions
/f web revokeall
/f web unlink
```

`/f panel link` creates a short-lived one-time onboarding code. Generate a new code if it expires; never share it publicly.

## Configuration and Maintenance

```text
/f config files [feature]
/f config validate [feature]
/f config report
/f config inspect <feature>
/f config reload [feature]
/f preset guide
/f preset preview <preset>
/f preset diff <preset>
/f preset apply <preset> confirm
/f saveall
/f compatibility report
```

Use full restart for JAR, dependency, license and major integration changes. Do not use Bukkit `/reload`.

title: Commands
excerpt: Click on any command for more details.
bodyMarkdown:
# Commands

> **Live command reference:** Commands can be disabled, aliased, permissioned, or cooled down in `commands.yml`. Use `/f help` for the command set active on your server and the [Practical Command Guide](command-guide.md) for common workflows.

Click on any command for more details.

## Configurable Definitions

### `/f upgrades admin definitions`

Lists loaded upgrade definitions, registered effect handlers, registered requirement handlers, and validation issues.

Permission: `factions.upgrades.admin`

### `/f upgrades admin inspect <id>`

Shows parsed upgrade levels, composite prices, and effects for one upgrade.

Permission: `factions.upgrades.admin`

### `/f upgrades admin reload`

Reloads `mxf/upgrade-definitions.yml` without restarting.

Permission: `factions.upgrades.admin`

### `/f missions`

Opens the faction mission GUI when menus are enabled, or shows the text fallback.

Permission: `factions.missions`

### `/f missions info <id>`

Shows mission objectives, progress, and rewards.

Permission: `factions.missions`

### `/f missions admin definitions`

Lists loaded mission definitions, registered objective/filter/reward handlers, and validation issues.

Permission: `factions.missions.admin`

### `/f missions admin reload`

Reloads `mxf/mission-definitions.yml` without restarting.

Permission: `factions.missions.admin`

> **Note:** Some commands take additional arguments. Arguments like <this> are *required*. Arguments like [this] are *optional*.

> **MxF YAML configuration:** MxF command enable switches, feature bindings, and generated alias defaults live in `plugins/Factions/commands.yml`. Global feature toggles live in `plugins/Factions/features.yml`; feature-specific command settings live under `plugins/Factions/mxf/*.yml`.

### Faction Member Commands

### /f join <faction>
Join a defined faction.

> **Requirements:** `factions.join`

### /f leave
Leave your faction.

> **Requirements:** `factions.leave`

### /f chat <mode>
Toggles chat modes or specify which channel you want to be in (public, alliance, faction, truce)

> **Requirements:** `factions.chat` node.

### /f togglealliancechat
Toggle ignoring alliance chat.

> **Requirements:** `factions.togglealliancechat` node.

### /f home
Teleports you to your faction's home.

> **Requirements:** `factions.home`

### /f warp [name] [password]
Teleports you to a warp, password optional. Opens the MxF warp GUI if no warp is defined. Aliases include `/f warps` and `/f fwarp`.

> **Requirements:** `factions.warp` node.

### /f warp menu
Explicitly opens the faction warp GUI when menus are enabled. Falls back to a text warp list when menu fallback is enabled.

> **Requirements:** `factions.warp` node.

### /f warp icon <warp>
Opens the Atlas-style warp icon picker for a faction warp. Alias: `/f warp seticon <warp>`.

> **Requirements:** `factions.setwarp` node. Must have faction permission to set warps.

### /f setwarpicon <warp>
Opens the same warp icon picker as `/f warp icon <warp>`.

> **Requirements:** `factions.setwarp` node. Must have faction permission to set warps.

### /f warpother <faction> [name] [password]
Teleports you to a faction's warp, password optional. Opens GUI if no warp defined.

> **Requirements:** `factions.warp` node. Permission to use that faction's warps.

### /f fly
Fly in your faction's territory. Disabled in combat.

> **Requirements:** `factions.fly` node.

### /f chest
Opens the configured shared faction chest using native MxF faction vault storage.

> **Requirements:** `factions.chest` node.

### /f chest menu
Explicitly opens the faction chest GUI when menus are enabled; otherwise falls back to the configured chest vault if text fallback is enabled.

> **Requirements:** `factions.chest` node.

### /f vault [number|menu|manager]
Opens a native shared faction vault, the vault selector, or the faction vault manager. Vault inventories are stored in `data/faction_vaults.yml` and do not require PlayerVaults.

> **Requirements:** `factions.vault` node. `/f vault manager` also requires `factions.vault.manager`.

### /f vaults
Opens the faction vault manager menu.

> **Requirements:** `factions.vault.manager` node.

### Faction Creation and Management

### /f create <name>
Create a faction with the given name.

> **Requirements:** `factions.create` node.

### /f disband
Disband your faction.

> **Requirements:** `factions.disband` node.

### /f tag <tag>
Change your faction's tag.

> **Requirements:** `factions.tag` node. Must be faction moderator or higher

### /f desc <description...>
Set your faction's new description.  

> **Requirements:** `factions.description` node. Must be faction moderator or higher

### /f sethome
Set your faction's home.

> **Requirements:** `factions.sethome` node.

### /f delhome <name>
Delete your faction's home

> **Requirements:** `factions.delhome` node.

### /f setwarp <name> [password]
Set a warp with an optional password to your location.

> **Requirements:** `factions.setwarp` node.

### /f delwarp <name>
Delete a warp

> **Requirements:** `factions.setwarp` node.

### /f announce <message...>
Creates an announcement sent to all faction members.  
Also saves the message for all current members who are offline, sending to them upon login.

> **Requirements:** `factions.announce` node. Must be moderator of your faction or higher to use.

### /f perms
Opens the faction permission menu when menus are enabled; otherwise lists faction permission selectors in chat.

> **Requirements:** `factions.permissions` node. Must be faction admin.

### /f perms menu
Opens the faction permission menu for roles inside the player's own faction. Relation and global selectors are staff-managed and are not exposed to faction leaders. Aliases include `/f perm menu` and `/f permissions menu`.

> **Requirements:** `factions.permissions` node. Must be faction admin.

### /f settings
Opens the faction settings menu when menus are enabled; otherwise shows faction setting status in chat. Aliases include `/f menu` and `/f control`.

> **Requirements:** `factions.settings` node. Must be in a faction.

### /f settings menu
Explicitly opens the faction settings menu with shortcuts for permissions, roster, alts, upgrades, shield, raids, corners, warps, and audit logs.

> **Requirements:** `factions.settings` node. Must be in a faction.

### /f corner
Opens the configured corner menu when menus are enabled; otherwise lists configured corners in chat. Alias: `/f corners`.

> **Requirements:** `factions.corner` node.

### /f corner list
Lists configured corners, claim status, owner, world, and chunk coordinates.

> **Requirements:** `factions.corner` node.

### /f corner claim <corner>
Confirms and purchases a configured corner area for your faction using the existing FactionsUUID claim/board logic. The cost, minimum role, radius, overwrite policy, and confirmation requirement are configured in `mxf/corners.yml`.

> **Requirements:** `factions.corner.claim` node. Must be in a normal faction when `mxf/corners.yml` has `claiming.require-faction=true`.

### /f corner teleport <corner>
Teleports to the configured corner location only when teleporting is enabled. Owner-only access and a separate teleport cost are configurable.

> **Requirements:** `factions.corner.teleport` node.

### /f corner menu
Explicitly opens the configured corner menu.

> **Requirements:** `factions.corner` node.

### /f upgrades
Opens the faction upgrade menu when menus are enabled; otherwise shows configured faction upgrades and current purchased levels in chat.

> **Requirements:** `factions.upgrades` node.

### /f upgrades menu
Opens the faction upgrade menu.

> **Requirements:** `factions.upgrades` node.

### /f upgrade info <upgrade>
Shows the current and next level cost/effects for a faction upgrade.

> **Requirements:** `factions.upgrades` node.

### /f upgrade buy <upgrade>
Purchases the next configured level for a faction upgrade.

> **Requirements:** `factions.upgrades` node. Requires faction membership and Vault economy when `upgrades.requireEconomy` is enabled.

### /f analytics
Shows cached faction analytics in chat.

Tracks faction members, roster size, alts, claims, TNT activity, raid activity, upgrades, shields, money movement, and latest cached FTop value.

> **Requirements:** `factions.analytics` node. Requires membership in a normal faction.

### /f analytics menu
Explicitly opens the faction analytics menu.

> **Requirements:** `factions.analytics` node.

### /f intelligence [faction]
Shows a cached faction intelligence report with score, risk level, strengths, weaknesses, recommendations, trends, and recent audit summary.

> **Requirements:** `factions.intelligence` node. Viewing another faction requires `factions.intelligence.others` or `factions.intelligence.admin`.

### /f intelligence menu [faction]
Opens the faction intelligence GUI using the MxF menu theme/effects system.

> **Requirements:** `factions.intelligence` node.

### /f intelligence guide
Explains the live scoring weights, risk thresholds, cache duration, and source data currently configured in `mxf/intelligence.yml`. See Faction Intelligence for the full scoring guide.

> **Requirements:** `factions.intelligence` node.

### /f intel
Alias for `/f intelligence`.

### /f report
Alias for your own faction intelligence report.

### /f season
Shows the active non-destructive season timeline overview.

> **Requirements:** `factions.season` node.

### /f season info
Shows detailed active season metadata, snapshot settings, and leaderboard size.

> **Requirements:** `factions.season` node.

### /f season top [page]
Shows the cached active season competitive leaderboard.

> **Requirements:** `factions.season.top` node.

### /f season history [page]
Shows ended and archived seasons with pagination.

> **Requirements:** `factions.season.history` node.

### /f season menu
Opens the season timeline GUI using the MxF menu theme/effects system.

> **Requirements:** `factions.season` node.

### /f hof
Shows archived season Hall of Fame champion records.

> **Requirements:** `factions.hof` node.

### /f halloffame
Alias for `/f hof`.

### /f season hof [page]
Shows archived Hall of Fame records through the season command.

> **Requirements:** `factions.season.hof` node.

### /f season hof menu
Opens the Hall of Fame GUI using the MxF menu theme/effects system.

> **Requirements:** `factions.season.hof` node.

### /f season rewards
Shows configured season reward tiers in preview-only mode.

> **Requirements:** `factions.season.rewards` node.

### /f season rewards preview
Shows which configured reward tiers would apply to the current active season leaderboard. Reward commands are not executed.

> **Requirements:** `factions.season.rewards` node.

### /f season rewards menu
Opens the reward tier GUI. The menu can toggle into current-season reward preview mode.

> **Requirements:** `factions.season.rewards` node.

### /f season start <id> <display name> confirm
Starts a new active season record and takes an initial snapshot. This does not reset factions, claims, money, TNT, upgrades, or player data.

> **Requirements:** `factions.season.admin` node.

### /f season end confirm
Ends the active season record after taking a final snapshot. This is non-destructive.

> **Requirements:** `factions.season.admin` node.

### /f season archive confirm
Archives the latest ended season record. This only affects season history retention.

> **Requirements:** `factions.season.admin` node.

### /f logs [category] [page]
Shows faction audit logs in chat. Running `/f logs` with menus enabled opens the audit log menu.

> **Requirements:** `factions.logs` node.

### /f logs menu [category]
Opens the faction audit log menu, optionally filtered by audit category.

> **Requirements:** `factions.logs` node.

### Member management

### /f defaultrole <role>
Sets your faction's default role for new members.

> **Requirements:** `factions.defaultrank` node. Must be faction leader.

### /f open
Toggle allowing anyone being able to join the faction.

> **Requirements:** `factions.open` node. Must be faction moderator or higher.

### /f invite <target>
Invite a player to your faction.

> **Requirements:** `factions.invite` node. Must be faction admin, or be granted `invite` perms in the faction.

### /f deinvite <target>
Revoke an invite from a player. If no player is defined, it will list all players with pending invites. 

> **Requirements:** `factions.deinvite` node. Must be faction admin, or be granted `invite` perms in the faction.

### /f deinvite <target>
Lists all players with pending invites. Click the names to revoke their invite.

> **Requirements:** `factions.showinvites` node. Must be faction admin, or be granted `invite` perms in the faction.

### /f roster
Opens your faction roster menu when menus are enabled; otherwise lists your roster in chat.

> **Requirements:** `factions.roster` node.

### /f roster list
Lists your faction roster in chat.

> **Requirements:** `factions.roster` node.

### /f roster menu
Opens your faction roster menu.

> **Requirements:** `factions.roster` node.

### /f roster add <player>
Adds an online or offline player to the faction roster.

> **Requirements:** `factions.roster.add` node. Must be faction moderator or higher.

### /f roster remove <player>
Removes a player from the faction roster.

> **Requirements:** `factions.roster.remove` node. Must be faction moderator or higher.

### /f roster rank <player> <rank>
Sets a text roster rank. Valid ranks are leader, coleader, mod, member, recruit, and alt.

> **Requirements:** `factions.roster.rank` node. Must be faction moderator or higher.

### /f alt
Opens your faction alt menu when menus are enabled; otherwise lists tracked faction alts in chat.

> **Requirements:** `factions.alts` node.

### /f alt list
Lists tracked faction alts in chat.

> **Requirements:** `factions.alts` node.

### /f alt menu
Opens the faction alt menu with player-head skins, online/location status, tracked alts, and pending invites. Left-click can visit an online tracked alt; authorized officers can right-click to remove or revoke.

> **Requirements:** `factions.alts` node.

### /f alt teleport <player>
Teleports to an online tracked alt account. Aliases: `/f alt tp` and `/f alt visit`.

> **Requirements:** `factions.alts.teleport` node.

### /f alt invite <player>
Invites a player to join the faction as an alt.

> **Requirements:** `factions.alts.invite` node. Must be faction moderator or higher.

### /f alt invites
Lists pending alt invites and expiry times.

> **Requirements:** `factions.alts` node.

### /f alt join <faction>
Joins a faction as an alt using an alt invite or open alt joining.

> **Requirements:** `factions.alts.join` node.

### /f alt kick <player>
Removes a tracked alt from the faction.

> **Requirements:** `factions.alts.kick` node. Must be faction moderator or higher.

### /f alt open
Opens faction alt joining.

> **Requirements:** `factions.alts.open` node. Must be faction moderator or higher.

### /f alt close
Closes faction alt joining.

> **Requirements:** `factions.alts.open` node. Must be faction moderator or higher.

### /f alt revoke <player>
Revokes a pending alt invite.

> **Requirements:** `factions.alts.revoke` node. Must be faction moderator or higher.

### /f title <player> [title]
Set a player's custom title. Will charge them if enabled.

> **Requirements:** `factions.title` node. Must be faction moderator or higher

### /f promote <name>
Promote a player by one rank.

> **Requirements:** `factions.promote` node.

### /f demote <name>
Demote a player by one rank.

> **Requirements:** `factions.promote` node.

### /f mod [name]
Promote a player in your faction to mod.

> **Requirements:** `factions.mod` node. Must be faction coleader or higher

### /f coleader [name]
Promote a player in your faction to coleader.

> **Requirements:** `factions.coleader` node. Must be faction leader

### /f admin <player>
Sets the new leader of your faction.

A server administrator can make any targeted player the leader of the faction that player is in.

> **Requirements:** `factions.admin` node. Must be admin of your faction to use. `factions.admin.any` node to change status of any player.

### /f kick <target>
Kicks a player from the faction.

> **Requirements:** `factions.kick` node. Must be faction admin, or be granted `kick` perms in the faction.

### /f ban <target>
Bans a player from the faction.

> **Requirements:** `factions.ban` node. Must be faction admin, or be granted `ban` perms in the faction.

### Territory

### /f claim [radius] [faction]
Defaults: radius = 1, faction = yours  
Claims one or more chunks for the given faction.  
Can only claim if the land is not claimed by another faction, or if the other faction has more land than power (and is not an ally).

> **Requirements:** Land cannot be owned by another faction (unless using power, the owning faction has more land than power, and the owning faction is not an ally) `factions.claim` node. `factions.claim.radius` node to claim a radius greater than 1. Must be faction admin, or be granted `territory` perms in the faction. To claim for safezone, must have `factions.managesafezone`. To claim for warzone, must have `factions.managewarzone`.

### /f unclaim [radius] [faction]
Defaults: radius = 1, faction = yours  
Returns one or more chunks of faction territory to the wilderness.  

> **Requirements:** `factions.unclaim` node. Must be faction admin, or be granted `territory` perms in the faction. To unclaim for safezone, must have `factions.managesafezone`. To unclaim for warzone, must have `factions.managewarzone`.

### /f autoclaim [faction]
Defaults: faction = yours  
Turns autoclaiming on or off. If on, any chunk you enter that you *can* claim, *will* be claimed up until
you reach your faction's limit.

> **Requirements:** `factions.autoclaim` node. Must be faction admin, or be granted `territory` perms in the faction. To claim for safezone, must have `factions.managesafezone`. To claim for warzone, must have `factions.managewarzone`.

### /f autounclaim [faction]
Defaults: faction = yours  
Turns autounclaiming on or off. If on, any chunk you enter that you *can* unclaim, *will* be unclaimed.

> **Requirements:** `factions.autoclaim` node. Must be faction admin, or be granted `territory` perms in the faction. To claim for safezone, must have `factions.managesafezone`. To claim for warzone, must have `factions.managewarzone`.

### /f claimat <world> <x> <z>
Attempts to claim a chunk in the given world at the given coordinates.  
The x and z values are *chunk coordinates*, not normal block coordinates.  
You can see chunk coordinates from `/f map`, for example.

> **Requirements:** `factions.claimat` node. Must be faction admin, or be granted `territory` perms in the faction.

### /f claimfill [amount] [faction]
Defaults: amount = config limit, faction = yours
Attempts to claim a number of blocks filling in an established shape of claims.  

> **Requirements:** `factions.claim.fill` node. Must be faction admin, or be granted `territory` perms in the faction. To claim for safezone, must have `factions.managesafezone`. To claim for warzone, must have `factions.managewarzone`.

### /f unclaimfill [amount] [faction]
Defaults: amount = config limit, faction = yours  
Attempts to unclaim all claims connected to the current claim.

> **Requirements:** `factions.unclaim.fill` node. Must be faction admin, or be granted `territory` perms in the faction. To claim for safezone, must have `factions.managesafezone`. To claim for warzone, must have `factions.managewarzone`.

### /f claimline [amount] [direction] [faction]
Defaults: amount = 1, direction = facing, faction = yours  
Attempts to claim a number of blocks in a line based on the direction given (or facing direction, if not specified).  
Acceptable directions: north, south, east, west.

> **Requirements:** `factions.claim.line` node. Must be faction admin, or be granted `territory` perms in the faction. To claim for safezone, must have `factions.managesafezone`. To claim for warzone, must have `factions.managewarzone`.

### /f unclaimall
The nuclear option. Removes all claims in your faction, returning them to the wilderness.

> **Requirements:** `factions.unclaimall` node. Must be faction admin, or be granted `territory` perms in the faction.

### /f safeunclaimall [world]
Defaults: world = all  
Removes all safezone claims in a given world, or in all worlds.

> **Requirements:** `factions.managesafezone` node.

### /f warunclaimall [world]
Defaults: world = all  
Removes all warzone claims in a given world, or in all worlds.

> **Requirements:** `factions.managewarzone` node.

### /f listclaims [world] [faction]
Defaults: world = current, faction = current  
Lists all coordinates of faction claims, merging attached claims into one coordinate with number of claims in parentheses.

> **Requirements:** `factions.listclaims` node. Must be faction admin, or be granted `listclaims` perms in the faction. To view other factions, must have`factions.listclaims.other`.

### MxF command styling

MxF-added command messages use `messages/shared.yml` plus the owning `messages/<feature>.yml` file. Root `messages.yml` and legacy language keys remain compatibility fallbacks.

MxF GUI commands use `menus/shared.yml` theme defaults for filler, close, pagination, back, and empty-state items unless the owning `menus/<feature>.yml` overrides them.

The shared MxF message renderer now applies MxF placeholders to command messages, styled wrappers, menu feedback, titles, and GUI text. Unknown placeholders render through `config.yml -> placeholders.fallback-text` when `replace-unknown` is enabled.

### MxF release polish behavior

- The root `/f` command now registers a Bukkit tab completer.
- Tab completion walks the existing FactionsUUID command tree and the Atlas/MxF merged command tree.
- Completions are value-aware where safe: players, cached faction players, faction tags, roles, relations, raid ids, raid claim ids, season ids/names, upgrade ids, corner ids, warp names, booleans, durations, page numbers, and common TNT amounts/radii.
- Incomplete commands now use a shared usage block with configurable TL/messages output: Usage, Example, Description, and Aliases.
- `/f help` uses the generated command registry when `commands.yml -> help.use-old-help` is false.
- Help categories include core, claims, economy, combat, raiding, TNT, analytics, administration, season, roster, alts, war, premium, and GUI.
- `/f tnt fill <radius> <amount>` now treats `<amount>` as the total requested transfer, distributes it across eligible dispenser/dropper inventories, and deducts only the TNT actually inserted from the faction bank.
- Generated YAML files now include standard MxF headers and `documentation` sections.
- Startup and `/f reload` validate MxF YAML for invalid materials, sounds, placeholders, menu sizes, page sizes, and duplicate slots. Warnings are logged without crashing the plugin.
- Shared menu theme items support `amount`, `glow`, and `hide-flags` in addition to material, data, slot, name, and lore.

### MxF player UX behavior

- Player-facing structured reports prefer GUI output when `config.yml -> ui.prefer-guis`, `menus/shared.yml -> enabled`, and `features.yml -> menus` are enabled.
- `/f help`, `/f top`, `/f pointstop`, `/f raid history`, `/f raid recap`, `/f season`, and `/f hof` keep console/text fallback through the `text`, `chat`, or `lines` argument.
- Staff-only UI details require `factions.ui.staffdetails`.
- Debug UI details require `factions.ui.debug`.
- Normal players should see friendly time/status text and compact numbers instead of internal ids, raw cache state, or exact timestamps.

### MxF configurable GUI behavior

- `menus/shared.yml` controls shared presentation and `menus/<feature>.yml` controls feature item layouts.
- Generic click actions include `CLOSE`, `REFRESH`, `SHOW_MESSAGE`, and `RUN_COMMAND`.
- Service-backed actions still use Java handlers so commands such as upgrade purchase, TNT fill, shield changes, warps, KOTH, and outposts keep their existing validation.
- `docs/gui-configuration.md` documents item fields, visibility conditions, actions, and validation warnings.
- `docs/ui-placeholders.md` lists common global and contextual placeholders for GUI text.

- `/f raidoutpost history [id] [page]` - shows bounded Raid Outpost history.
- `/f raidoutpost admin create <id>` - creates a YAML definition shell.
- `/f raidoutpost admin delete <id>` - deletes a definition and safely stops any active session.
- `/f raidoutpost admin setouter|setbreach|setentry|setcore <id>` - saves the current WorldEdit selection to a region.
- `/f raidoutpost admin setteleport <id>` - saves the player's location as the teleport point.
- `/f raidoutpost admin start <id>` - manually starts an event.
- `/f raidoutpost admin stop <id>` - cancels an active event.
- `/f raidoutpost admin forcapture <id> <faction>` - completes an event for a faction through the normal reward/reset path.
- `/f raidoutpost admin reset <id>` - runs the configured reset profile.
- `/f raidoutpost admin snapshot <id>` - captures a fresh block snapshot for validation.
- `/f raidoutpost admin setduration <id> <seconds>` - updates event duration.
- `/f raidoutpost admin enable|disable <id>` - toggles a definition.
- `/f raidoutpost admin inspect <id>` - shows staff diagnostics.
- `/f raidoutpost admin reload` - reloads Raid Outpost YAML.
- `/f raidoutpost admin save` - saves runtime Raid Outpost state.

## Investigation And Rollback

- `/f investigate` - opens the staff investigation dashboard for players when menus are enabled; console uses text fallback.
- `/f investigate recent [category] [page]` - shows recent normalized investigation events.
- `/f investigate faction <faction> [page]` - shows a faction timeline.
- `/f investigate player <player> [page]` - shows a player timeline.
- `/f investigate event <eventId>` - shows complete event details.
- `/f investigate related <eventId>` - shows related timeline events.
- `/f investigate transactions <faction>` - shows faction transaction records.
- `/f investigate tnt|points|money|upgrades|rewards <faction>` - filters transaction categories.
- `/f investigate search <text>` - searches indexed event text and metadata.
- `/f investigate case ...` - manages internal staff investigation workspaces.
- `/f investigate menu` - opens the investigation event GUI.
- `/f investigate rebuild` - rebuilds the normalized index from authoritative sources.
- `/f investigate reload` - reloads investigation YAML.
- `/f investigate export event|faction ...` - writes a local investigation report.
- `/f inv`, `/f history`, and `/f timeline` alias `/f investigate`.
- `/f rollback preview <eventId>` - previews a safe rollback without mutating data.
- `/f rollback select|deselect <eventId>` - manages a multi-event rollback selection.
- `/f rollback selection` - lists selected events.
- `/f rollback clear` - clears selected events.
- `/f rollback preview selected` - previews the selected event group.
- `/f rollback confirm <token> <reason>` - executes a one-time preview token.
- `/f rollback history` - lists rollback records.
- `/f rollback detail <rollbackId>` - shows execution details.
- `/f rollback undo preview <rollbackId>` - previews undoing a rollback.
- `/f rollback undo confirm <token> <reason>` - executes a safe undo token.
- `/f rollback menu` - opens rollback history GUI.

## Reward Economy And Shop

- `/f shop` - opens the default MxF shop GUI or text fallback.
- `/f shop menu [shop]` - opens a configured shop GUI.
- `/f shop list [shop] [page]` - lists configured shop items.
- `/f shop buy [shop] <item> confirm` - purchases a configured shop item.
- `/f shop history [page]` - shows relevant purchase history.
- `/f shop admin reload` - reloads reward economy YAML.
- `/f shop admin validate` - shows shop/reward definition diagnostics.
- `/f shop admin inspect <id>` - inspects a shop or reward.
- `/f shop admin refund <purchaseId> [reason]` - marks a purchase refunded and restores reversible currencies.
- `/f pointshop` - opens the configured Season Points shop without creating a second purchase backend. Aliases: `/f pshop` and `/f spstore`.
- `/f rewards` - lists configured reward definitions.
- `/f rewards validate` - shows reward economy diagnostics.
- `/f rewards inspect <id>` - inspects a shop or reward definition.
- `/f rewards give <player> <reward>` - grants a configured reward manually.
- `/f rewards refund <purchaseId> [reason]` - marks a purchase refunded.

## Web Dashboard

- `/f web` or `/f panel` - shows MxF web integration status.
- `/f panel link` - creates a short-lived faction onboarding code; click it to place it into the Minecraft 1.8.8 chat input for copying.
- `/f web link <code>` - completes the legacy website-first identity flow only when the legacy embedded backend is active.
- `/f web unlink` - revokes linked web accounts for your Minecraft UUID.
- `/f web sessions` - lists active web dashboard sessions.
- `/f web revokeall` - revokes your active web dashboard sessions.
- `/f web admin status` - shows API binding, accounts, sessions, and masked credential state.
- `/f web admin diagnose` - shows web API diagnostics and security warnings.
- `/f web admin unlink <player>` - staff unlink for a player.
- `/f web admin revoke <player>` - staff session revocation for a player.
- `/f web admin reload` - reloads web YAML and restarts the API binding if enabled.

## Configuration

- `/f config files [feature]` - lists registered files, owners, state, and reload policy.
- `/f config validate [feature]` - shows structured YAML validation results.
- `/f config report` - summarizes loaded/missing/invalid/fallback files and migrations.
- `/f config inspect <feature>` - shows ownership and legacy migration sources.
- `/f config reload [feature]` - reloads all configuration or an indicated service family.
- `/f config migrate status|preview|report` - inspects copy-only migration state.
- `/f config migrate run confirm` - runs pending safe migrations after explicit confirmation.
- `/f preset guide` - explains preset scope, previews, backups, confirmation, and rollback safety.
- `/f preset list|preview|diff` - inspects included YAML presets.
- `/f preset apply <preset> confirm` - backs up and applies a preset.
- `/f preset backups` - lists restorable preset backups.
- `/f preset rollback <backup> confirm` - restores an earlier preset backup.

## Competitive Interfaces

- `/f top` or `/f ftop` - opens the cached FTop podium. The first three factions use fixed podium slots; remaining factions use configured paginated slots. Click a faction for its value, movement, members, and claim details.
- `/f refreshtop` - safely rebuilds the cached leaderboard, subject to its configured permission and cooldown.
- `/f intelligence` or `/f intel` - opens the faction Intelligence GUI. Use `/f intelligence text` for chat output and `/f intelligence guide` for scoring details.
- `/f rollback` or `/f rollback menu` - opens rollback history for authorized staff. Execution still requires preview and a confirmation token.
- `/f holograms status` - shows the selected provider and configured/active display counts.
- `/f holograms refresh` - refreshes MxF-managed displays.
- `/f holograms reload` - reloads `mxf/holograms.yml` and safely replaces the refresh task/displays.

In `/f alts`, an authorized officer can left-click an active alt head to bring its online player or managed Citizens NPC to the officer after the configured warmup. Right-click retains the configured remove/revoke action.

title: Compatibility
excerpt: MxF Factions is built as one Java 8 bytecode jar with a runtime compatibility layer for 1.8.8 through modern Spigot/Paper targets.
bodyMarkdown:
# Compatibility

MxF Factions is built as one Java 8 bytecode jar with a runtime compatibility layer for 1.8.8 through modern Spigot/Paper targets.

## Command

Use:

```text
/f compatibility
/f compat
```

This shows the detected server version, compatibility family, Java version, supported capabilities, and integration adapter status.

## YAML

The compatibility settings generate at:

```text
plugins/Factions/mxf/compatibility.yml
```

The file reloads through:

```text
/f reload
```

## Materials

For menu and MxF feature configs, prefer dual material syntax:

```yaml
material: "RED_STAINED_GLASS_PANE|STAINED_GLASS_PANE:14"
```

The first value is the modern material. The second value is the legacy fallback. The optional number after `:` is legacy item data for 1.8-era servers.

## Sounds

Sounds can use modern and legacy fallbacks:

```yaml
sound: "UI_BUTTON_CLICK|CLICK"
```

Invalid sounds are ignored with a warn-once diagnostic when enabled.

## Runtime Support

The adapter layer covers high-risk MxF surfaces such as menus, sounds, titles/actionbars, FTop materials, spawners, world heights, outpost buffs, and YAML validation.

Actual support still depends on runtime testing and compatible third-party dependency jars for the target server version. See `MXF_COMPATIBILITY_TEST_MATRIX.md` and `MXF_REMAINING_VERSION_RISKS.md` in the project root.

title: Configuration File Map
excerpt: All paths below are relative to `plugins/Factions/`. Missing managed YAML files are generated by the current build when their subsystem initializes.
bodyMarkdown:
# Configuration File Map

All paths below are relative to `plugins/Factions/`. Missing managed YAML files are generated by the current build when their subsystem initializes.

## Root Files

| File | Owns | Reload policy |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `config.yml` | License key, shared MxF behavior and placeholder policy. | Live except license changes; restart for license. |
| `commands.yml` | Command toggles, aliases, permission overlays, descriptions, usage, cooldowns. | Live. |
| `features.yml` | Global feature hard switches. | Live; verify owned tasks stop/start once. |
| `permissions.yml` | Permission labels and mappings. | Live. |
| `integrations.yml` | Optional hook enablement. | Restart recommended. |
| `diagnostics.yml` | Startup, reload, compatibility, validation diagnostics. | Live. |
| `messages.yml` | Legacy compatibility message overlay. | Live. |
| `lang.yml` | FactionsUUID locale fallback. | Live, but split message files take precedence. |

## Menu Files

Shared behavior is in `menus/shared.yml`. Feature-owned files include:

```text
menus/faction.yml             menus/members.yml
menus/missions.yml            menus/upgrades.yml
menus/koth.yml                menus/outposts.yml
menus/raid-outposts.yml       menus/season.yml
menus/shop.yml                menus/ftop.yml
menus/investigations.yml      menus/rollback.yml
menus/raid.yml                menus/tnt.yml
menus/shields.yml             menus/warps.yml
menus/vaults.yml              menus/analytics.yml
menus/intelligence.yml        menus/audit.yml
menus/war-center.yml          menus/raid-alerts.yml
menus/raid-recaps.yml
```

These files own presentation only: title, size, slots, item states, names, lore, materials, sounds, effects, navigation, and safe menu actions.

## Message Files

`messages/shared.yml` owns common formatting. Feature files include:

```text
messages/faction.yml          messages/missions.yml
messages/upgrades.yml         messages/koth.yml
messages/outposts.yml         messages/raid-outposts.yml
messages/season.yml           messages/shop.yml
messages/ftop.yml             messages/investigations.yml
messages/rollback.yml         messages/raids.yml
messages/tnt.yml              messages/shields.yml
messages/members.yml          messages/warps.yml
messages/audit.yml            messages/analytics.yml
messages/intelligence.yml     messages/web.yml
```

## Feature Files

| Files | System |
| --- | --- |
| `mxf/audit.yml` | Audit categories, retention, visibility, pagination. |
| `mxf/tnt.yml` | TNT capacity, deposits, withdrawals, fill/siphon limits and scanning. |
| `mxf/ftop.yml` | Cache interval, values, worlds, pagination and manual refresh. |
| `mxf/timers.yml`, `mxf/shields.yml` | Grace, SOTW, shield windows and restrictions. |
| `mxf/raids.yml`, `mxf/raid-alerts.yml`, `mxf/raid-recaps.yml` | Raid sessions, reservations, detection, notification, history and recaps. |
| `mxf/raid-outposts.yml`, `mxf/raid-outpost-definitions.yml` | Raid Outpost engine and arenas. |
| `mxf/koth.yml`, `mxf/koth-definitions.yml` | KOTH engine and arenas. Older `mxf/koths.yml` may remain as a migration source. |
| `mxf/outposts.yml`, `mxf/outpost-definitions.yml` | Outpost engine and definitions. |
| `mxf/missions.yml`, `mxf/mission-definitions.yml` | Mission engine and content. |
| `mxf/upgrades.yml`, `mxf/upgrade-definitions.yml` | Upgrade engine and content. |
| `mxf/rewards.yml`, `mxf/reward-definitions.yml` | Reward behavior and reusable reward actions. |
| `mxf/shop.yml`, `mxf/shop-definitions.yml` | Shop behavior and catalogs. |
| `mxf/seasons.yml`, `mxf/season-points.yml`, `mxf/season-rewards.yml` | Season lifecycle, points, Hall of Fame and rewards. |
| `mxf/competitive-rules.yml`, `mxf/rule-definitions.yml` | Discipline engine and server-defined rules. |
| `mxf/investigations.yml`, `mxf/rollback.yml` | Investigation indexing and safe rollback. |
| `mxf/roster.yml`, `mxf/alts.yml` | Roster and alt limits/workflows. |
| `mxf/warps.yml`, `mxf/vaults.yml`, `mxf/corners.yml` | Faction utility systems. |
| `mxf/analytics.yml`, `mxf/intelligence.yml` | Tracking, snapshots, score weights and cache. |
| `mxf/war-center.yml`, `mxf/visuals.yml` | Tactical dashboard and shared visual language. |
| `mxf/compatibility.yml` | Material, sound, particle and server-family adapters. |
| `mxf/web.yml`, `mxf/web-dashboard.yml`, `mxf/web-permissions.yml`, `mxf/web-theme.yml`, `mxf/web-backend.yml` | Web API, dashboard, authorization, theme, and backend bridge. |

## Presets

Packaged preset manifests generate under:

```text
presets/competitive/preset.yml
presets/hcf/preset.yml
presets/classic/preset.yml
presets/lite/preset.yml
presets/development/preset.yml
```

Preset application creates snapshots under `backups/`.

## Legacy Compatibility

The following may remain from FactionsUUID and can still own classic core behavior:

```text
config/main.conf
config/permissions.conf
config/translations.conf
config/dynmap.conf
```

Do not assume `config/main.conf` owns an MxF system merely because it was historically the main FactionsUUID config. New MxF features use YAML as listed above.

## Runtime Data

Core stores:

- `data/factions.json`
- `data/players.json`
- `data/board.json`

Additional stores include audit logs, rosters, alts, missions, upgrades, timers, shields, raids, raid claims/alerts/recaps/outposts, seasons, Season Points, Hall of Fame, analytics, intelligence, competitive cases, investigations, rollback history, purchases, warp icons, native vaults, and web link/session/profile data.

Runtime JSON is not configuration. Back it up, do not publish it, and do not edit it while the server is running. See Data and Backups.

title: Configuration Guide
excerpt: MxF Factions uses ownership-based YAML. Global controls live at the plugin root, presentation is split by feature, and gameplay definitions live under `mxf/`.
bodyMarkdown:
# Configuration Guide

MxF Factions uses ownership-based YAML. Global controls live at the plugin root, presentation is split by feature, and gameplay definitions live under `mxf/`.

## Configuration Layers

| Layer | Purpose | Examples |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Root YAML | License, global feature/command controls, integrations, diagnostics. | `config.yml`, `features.yml`, `commands.yml` |
| Feature YAML | Gameplay behavior, prices, timers, limits, definitions. | `mxf/tnt.yml`, `mxf/seasons.yml`, `mxf/mission-definitions.yml` |
| Menus | Titles, sizes, slots, icons, lore, navigation, sounds, theme states. | `menus/shared.yml`, `menus/missions.yml` |
| Messages | Player-facing chat/title/actionbar text and shared formatting. | `messages/shared.yml`, `messages/raids.yml` |
| Legacy compatibility | Original FactionsUUID settings still used by core behavior. | `config/main.conf`, `lang.yml` |
| Runtime data | Factions, claims, purchases, progress, event state. Do not hand-edit live. | `data/*.json` |

## Find the Right File

- Is the whole feature enabled? Check `features.yml`.
- Is a command enabled, renamed, permissioned, or cooled down? Check `commands.yml`.
- Is it a gameplay limit, price, timer, reward, objective, or rule? Check `mxf/<feature>.yml` or its definition file.
- Is it a GUI title, slot, material, name, lore, button, or sound? Check `menus/<feature>.yml`.
- Is it player-facing text? Check `messages/<feature>.yml`.
- Is it a third-party hook? Check `integrations.yml` and the feature YAML.
- Is it a classic FactionsUUID power/claim/protection option? It may remain in a legacy compatibility file.

The full ownership list is in Configuration Files.

## Safe Edit Workflow

1. Back up the file and relevant runtime data.
2. Change one logical area at a time.
3. Keep YAML indentation consistent and quote strings containing special characters.
4. Run:

```text
/f config validate
```

5. Reload the smallest owning scope:

```text
/f config reload menus
/f config reload messages
/f reload missions
```

6. Reopen menus after reload; already open inventories are not rewritten in place.
7. Trigger both the success and failure path.
8. Restart staging once to prove persistence and task lifecycle.

Use `/f config status` to inspect registered files and reload policy. `integrations.yml`, JAR changes, dependency changes, and license changes are safest with a full restart.

## Feature and Command Controls

`features.yml` is the global hard switch for MxF systems. Disabling a feature should remove or disable its command/menu surface and stop its owned tasks.

`commands.yml` controls command availability and metadata. Depending on the command, entries can provide enabled state, aliases, permission overlay, description, usage, and cooldown. Use a unique alias and test tab completion after changes.

Do not use command aliases to imitate another plugin's root command without testing conflicts.

## Message Resolution

MxF resolves text in this order:

1. `messages/<feature>.yml`
2. Root `messages.yml` compatibility value
3. `lang.yml` legacy locale value
4. Java emergency default

Strings and string lists are supported. Legacy `&` colors and documented placeholders are supported. Invalid or unresolved values follow the placeholder policy in `config.yml`.

See UI Placeholders, Placeholders, and PlaceholderAPI.

## Menu Configuration

Shared theme, fillers, navigation, sounds, feedback, and action safety belong in `menus/shared.yml`. Feature layouts belong in `menus/<feature>.yml`.

Menu sizes must be 9, 18, 27, 36, 45, or 54. Slots are zero-based. A typical item is:

```yaml
items:
  example:
enabled: true
slot: 10
material: "DIAMOND|DIAMOND"
legacy-data: 0
amount: 1
name: "&bExample"
lore:
  - "&7Faction: &f{faction}"
glow: false
```

Gameplay prices and rewards do not belong in menu lore alone. Put the real value in the owning feature/definition YAML and render it with placeholders.

See GUI Configuration for actions, themes, sounds, compatibility syntax, and safety allowlists.

## Definitions

Definitions create server content without compiling Java:

- `mxf/mission-definitions.yml`
- `mxf/upgrade-definitions.yml`
- `mxf/reward-definitions.yml`
- `mxf/shop-definitions.yml`
- `mxf/rule-definitions.yml`
- `mxf/koth-definitions.yml`
- `mxf/outpost-definitions.yml`
- `mxf/raid-outpost-definitions.yml`

Definitions are data, not arbitrary code. Handler types must exist in the plugin registry. Unknown types fail closed and should produce validation diagnostics.

## Presets

Included presets are `competitive`, `hcf`, `classic`, `lite`, and `development`.

```text
/f preset list
/f preset preview <preset>
/f preset diff <preset>
/f preset apply <preset> confirm
/f preset backups
/f preset rollback <backup> confirm
```

Presets apply specific YAML paths and create a backup. They do not wipe factions or copy runtime JSON. Always review `diff` before applying.

## Legacy and Modern Material Names

Cross-version item/sound fields can use modern-to-legacy fallback syntax:

```yaml
material: "RED_STAINED_GLASS_PANE|STAINED_GLASS_PANE:14"
sound: "UI_BUTTON_CLICK|CLICK"
```

Invalid materials fall back safely where supported. Invalid sounds are ignored and can warn once through diagnostics.

## What Not to Edit Live

- `data/*.json`
- The plugin JAR
- Dependency JARs
- Generated license/install identity
- Major integration binding/network settings
- Large legacy configuration sections

Stop the server for those operations and keep a matching backup.

title: Permission Contexts
excerpt: Presently, only LuckPerms is supported for this feature. To get more information on how to use
bodyMarkdown:
# Permission Contexts

Presently, only [LuckPerms](https://luckperms.net/) is supported for this feature. To get more information on how to use
contexts in LuckPerms, click [this link for their documentation](https://luckperms.net/wiki/Context). You need to be
running at least LuckPerms version 5.1.0 for MxF Factions to utilize this feature.

## Contexts Offered

### Faction ID

`factionsuuid:faction-id`  
**Possible values**: Any number matching an existing faction's ID  
**Description**: The ID of the faction the player is in. If not in a faction, they get the 
wilderness ID which is 0. Presently you need to check `data/factions.json` for the faction ID. 
The tag is not used because factions could change tag and that would be painful to juggle.

### Is peaceful?

`factionsuuid:is-peaceful`  
**Possible values**: true, false  
**Description**: True if the player is in a peaceful faction.

### Is permanent?

`factionsuuid:is-permanent`  
**Possible values**: true, false  
**Description**: True if the player is in a permanent faction.

### Territory relation

`factionsuuid:territory-relation`  
**Possible values**: member, ally, truce, neutral, enemy  
**Description**: The player's relation to the land they are in. Only one value at a time. Could be used to, for example, 
prevent players in enemy territory from using particular features of another plugin that are permission-based (not 
just limited to commands).

### Faction role at least
 
`factionsuuid:role-at-least`  
**Possible values**: admin, coleader, moderator, normal, recruit  
**Description**: If a player is a recruit, they will only have the recruit value. If moderator, they will have moderator,
normal, and recruit. Could be used to, for example, grant a permission node only to faction admins.

### Faction role at most

`factionsuuid:role-at-most`  
**Possible values**: admin, coleader, moderator, normal, recruit  
**Description**: If a player is an admin, they will only have the admin value. If moderator, they will have moderator,
coleader, and admin. Could be used to, for example, grant a permission node only to normal members and recruits.

title: Custom Missions
excerpt: Faction missions are definition-driven and stored in `plugins/Factions/mxf/mission-definitions.yml`. Runtime progress is stored in `plugins/Factions/data/missions.json`.
bodyMarkdown:
# Custom Missions

Faction missions are definition-driven and stored in `plugins/Factions/mxf/mission-definitions.yml`. Runtime progress is stored in `plugins/Factions/data/missions.json`.

The current mission engine listens to normalized events from existing systems:

- TNT deposit/withdraw/fill/siphon
- Upgrade purchases
- KOTH captures
- Outpost captures
- Outpost interval rewards
- Claim chunks
- Unclaim chunks

Useful commands:

- `/f missions` opens the GUI or text fallback.
- `/f missions info <id>` shows objective progress.
- `/f missions admin definitions` lists loaded definitions and validation issues.
- `/f missions admin inspect <id>` shows parsed objectives/rewards.
- `/f missions admin reload` reloads mission definitions.

Live QA definitions used during hardening:

```yml
missions:
  test_deposit_tnt:
enabled: true
category: DAILY
objective:
  type: DEPOSIT_TNT
  target: 10
rewards:
  entries:
    - type: SEASON_POINTS
      amount: 5
  test_claim_chunks:
enabled: true
category: DAILY
objective:
  type: CLAIM_CHUNKS
  target: 3
rewards:
  entries:
    - type: SEASON_POINTS
      amount: 5
```

Hardening behavior:

- Unsupported objectives are skipped and reported.
- Missing mission chain references are reported and removed from the active registry.
- Circular mission chains are reported and removed from the active registry.
- Malformed YAML keeps the previous valid registry when possible.
- Existing progress remains in `data/missions.json` if a definition is removed, and admin diagnostics report orphan progress.

See `examples/mission-definitions-example.yml`.

## Beta Default Pack

The generated default mission pack now avoids unsupported placeholder-only objectives. Active examples use registered handlers such as TNT deposit, chunk claims, upgrade purchases, KOTH captures, and Outpost captures.

If `mxf/mission-definitions.yml` is missing or has no `missions` section entries, the loader writes beta-safe examples. Existing server-owner definitions are preserved.

title: Custom Upgrades
excerpt: MxF faction upgrades are definition-driven. Server owners create upgrades in `plugins/Factions/mxf/upgrade-definitions.yml`; purchase behavior and global feature toggles stay in `plugins/Factions/mxf/upgrades.yml`.
bodyMarkdown:
# Custom Upgrades

MxF faction upgrades are definition-driven. Server owners create upgrades in `plugins/Factions/mxf/upgrade-definitions.yml`; purchase behavior and global feature toggles stay in `plugins/Factions/mxf/upgrades.yml`.

Definitions support safe IDs, display metadata, requirements, composite prices, and registered effects. Invalid definitions are skipped and reported by `/f upgrades admin definitions`.

Useful commands:

- `/f upgrades` opens the GUI or text fallback.
- `/f upgrade buy <id>` buys the next level.
- `/f upgrades admin definitions` lists loaded definitions and validation issues.
- `/f upgrades admin inspect <id>` shows parsed levels/effects.
- `/f upgrades admin reload` reloads upgrade definitions without restarting.

Live QA definitions used during hardening:

```yml
upgrades:
  test_tnt_capacity:
enabled: true
category: STORAGE
display:
  name: "&cTest TNT Capacity"
  material: TNT
levels:
  1:
    price:
      money: 100
    effects:
      - type: ADD_TNT_CAPACITY
        amount: 1000
  test_warp_limit:
enabled: true
category: UTILITY
display:
  name: "&bTest Warp Limit"
  material: ENDER_PEARL
levels:
  1:
    price:
      money: 100
    effects:
      - type: ADD_WARP_LIMIT
        amount: 1
```

Hardening behavior:

- Unsupported effects are skipped and reported.
- Missing `REQUIRED_UPGRADE_LEVEL` references are reported and removed from the active registry.
- Circular upgrade dependencies are reported and removed from the active registry.
- Malformed YAML keeps the previous valid registry when possible.

See `examples/upgrade-definitions-example.yml`.

## Beta Default Pack

The generated upgrade definitions now focus on effects consumed by existing systems:

- TNT capacity.
- Claim limit.
- Warp expansion.
- FTop multiplier.

Conceptual effects stay out of active defaults until their subsystem consumers are wired and tested.

title: Data, Backups, and Recovery
excerpt: The plugin data folder contains configuration, faction state, economy-adjacent state, event progress, evidence, sessions, and web identities. Treat it as production data.
bodyMarkdown:
# Data, Backups, and Recovery

The plugin data folder contains configuration, faction state, economy-adjacent state, event progress, evidence, sessions, and web identities. Treat it as production data.

## What to Back Up

Always back up the entire `plugins/Factions/` folder. A complete server backup should also include:

- All worlds.
- Vault economy provider data.
- Permission plugin data.
- WorldGuard/WorldEdit region data if used.
- Placeholder/web backend configuration relevant to the deployment.
- The exact plugin and dependency JARs or their hashes.

## Runtime Stores

Core FactionsUUID:

- `data/factions.json`: faction identity, members and faction state.
- `data/players.json`: FPlayer state.
- `data/board.json`: chunk ownership.

MxF services add stores for audit logs, TNT-adjacent tracking, rosters, alts, vaults, missions, upgrades, timers, shields, raids, alerts, recaps, event state, seasons, points, Hall of Fame, analytics, intelligence, purchases, competitive cases, investigations, rollbacks, and web links/sessions/profiles.

Do not publish runtime JSON in a documentation bundle or bug report. Redact UUIDs, IPs, license values, web tokens, evidence, notes, and secrets.

## Backup Schedule

Recommended minimum:

- Daily full `plugins/Factions/` backup.
- Backup before every JAR, dependency, preset, definition, or large YAML change.
- Backup before season transitions, reward execution, major event resets, and bulk staff actions.
- Retain at least one off-machine copy.
- Test restoration, not only creation.

## Consistent Backup Procedure

1. Announce maintenance.
2. Run `/f saveall`.
3. Stop the server cleanly.
4. Copy the folder and record timestamp/version/hash.
5. Start the server.
6. Verify clean startup and one representative faction.

Live filesystem copies can capture mismatched JSON while writes are in progress. A stopped-server backup is the recovery standard.

## Corrupt File Quarantine

Many MxF services rename unreadable JSON to a timestamped `.corrupt-*` file and start a clean store instead of overwriting evidence. If this happens:

1. Stop the server.
2. Preserve the corrupt file and logs.
3. Do not repeatedly restart and create more empty state.
4. Restore the affected file from the matching complete backup, or contact support for repair.
5. Verify related stores before reopening.

## Restore Procedure

1. Stop the server.
2. Preserve the current failed folder.
3. Restore the plugin JAR and complete data folder from the same backup point.
4. Restore external economy/permissions/world data when the incident involved them.
5. Start in staging or maintenance mode.
6. Verify faction count, member count, board claims, balances, TNT, upgrades, mission progress, season state, events, and audit history.

Never mix arbitrary stores from different timestamps unless you understand their relationships.

## Save and Reload Are Different

- `/f saveall` flushes current runtime state.
- `/f reload` reloads configuration and service definitions.
- A restart reloads code, dependencies, integrations, license state, configuration, and runtime data.

Reload is not a backup, and saveall does not validate YAML.

bodyMarkdown:
# Production Default Content

MxF Factions ships a complete first-run reference configuration. Packaged definition templates are installed only when their target file does not exist, so server-owner edits are never overwritten.

## Included Catalogs

- 52 faction missions across daily, weekly, seasonal, repeatable, elite, and legendary progression.
- 10 multi-level upgrades using live TNT capacity, claim limit, warp limit, and FTop multiplier consumers.
- 24 reusable rewards and bundles using supported money, faction money, Season Points, XP, items, and TNT actions.
- 8 populated shops covering personal, faction, season, daily, weekly, featured, limited, and staff QA workflows.
- 8 KOTH arena definitions with distinct timers and reward profiles.
- 8 Outposts with economy, TNT, combat, experience, and season-oriented rewards and buffs.
- 6 Raid Outpost templates covering a fortress, bridge, castle, temple, vault, and stronghold.

## Safe Defaults

- KOTH schedules are disabled until map coordinates are reviewed.
- Five advanced Raid Outpost templates are disabled until regions and reset behavior are configured.
- Third-party crates, tokens, titles, cosmetics, and external commands are not assumed.
- Definitions use only objective and effect handlers that are registered in the current runtime.
- Missing options continue to merge through the existing YAML lifecycle while preserving configured values.

## FTop Values

Default block and spawner values include diamond, emerald, gold, iron, obsidian, Creeper, Blaze, and Iron Golem assets. Empty leaderboards render a deliberate empty state rather than an `N/A` faction entry.

See `COMPLETE_PRODUCT_POLISH_REPORT.md` for the full catalog, compatibility notes, and intentionally deferred engine extensions.

## Existing Beta Installs

Servers still using the exact untouched two-mission or three-upgrade beta template are upgraded to the production catalog on startup. MxF creates a timestamped `pre-production` backup before replacement. A changed description, changed id set, or additional custom definition prevents this migration, so owner content is preserved.

The mission GUI groups the catalog into All, Daily, Weekly, Seasonal, and Elite tabs. Upgrade effects are presented as player benefits rather than registry identifiers.

title: Definitions
excerpt: Word | Meaning
bodyMarkdown:
# Definitions

Word | Meaning
--- | ---
Faction | A group of players linked together on a team.
Relation | The connection between a set of teams (ally, truce, enemy, neutral).
Ally | A faction that is with you and can be trusted.
Truce | A temporary alliance between two Factions.
Enemy | A faction that you or your faction are against.
Neutral | The default relation between two factions.
Chunks | In Minecraft, this is 16 blocks wide and 16 blocks long on the X and Z-axis. This stretches to the bottom of the world.
Claims | The amount of land (chunks) you take up when using the command /f claim.
Territory | The area or claims when using /f map. The more power, and members you have the more land you can claim.
Over-Claim | This is when a faction has more land than power. Factions are allowed to claim on a factions territory. 
Power | The influence that a faction has. Can be used to claim land. You can lose power if a player dies, and that can lead to being over-claimed.
Description | What players get to know about the faction.
Balance | The amount of money the player has in or outside of the faction. There are player balances and faction balances.
Warp | The destination or a location that can be used to be teleported to. An alternative way of travel.
Raid | The word one uses to destroy, loot or obliterate a faction using force, strategy, and or TNT. 
Permission | The authority to do something.
TNT Bank | TNT that can be stored as part of the faction. Can be filled into dispensers or siphoned from them.

title: Developers
excerpt: Repository: `https://ci.ender.zone/plugin/repository/everything/`
bodyMarkdown:
# Developers

> **This page contains information only useful to plugin developers:**

### Maven details:  
Repository: `https://ci.ender.zone/plugin/repository/everything/`  
groupId: `com.massivecraft`  
artifactId: `Factions`  
version: `1.6.9.5-U0.6.35`

## FLocation
FLocation is a Chunk wrapper. If you ever want to deal with the map, claimed land, or something similar, you'll need to
convert a Location or Chunk into an FLocation, both of which are super easy :)

**Getting from a Bukkit Location.**
```java
FLocation flocation = new FLocation(location);
```

**Getting from a Chunk**
```java
// Broken apart just to avoid making an overly wide example
String worldName = chunk.getWorld().getName();
int x = chunk.getX();
int z = chunk.getZ();

FLocation flocation = new FLocation(worldName, x, z);
```

## FPlayers
There is always **1** FPlayer object for each player that's been on the server, including online ones. It's very easy to
get the associated FPlayer if you already have the Bukkit Player or their UUID.

**By Bukkit Player**
```java
FPlayer fplayer = FPlayers.getInstance().getByPlayer(player);
```

**By UUID**
```java
FPlayer fplayer = FPlayers.getInstance().getById(uuid.toString());
```

**Get Role**
```java
Role fplayerRole = fplayer.getRole();
``` 

## Factions
There are multiple ways you can get a Faction.

**Most common is by name (aka tag)**
```java
Faction faction = Factions.getInstance().getByTag("name");
```

If you have a FLocation, you can get the Faction that owns it (including Wilderness, Warzone, and Safezone)
```java
Faction faction = Board.getInstance().getFactionAt(fLocation);
```
