# Faction Dashboard

The default dashboard mirrors the MxF dark-purple panel style: left navigation, faction header, statistic cards, activity widgets, top members, raid status, shop preview, and right-side faction information.

The dashboard is driven by plugin API data:

- faction identity and description
- leader, claims, power, and home visibility policy
- member and online counts
- faction TNT
- Vault balance when available
- FTop value and rank from cached snapshots
- Season Points and rank
- shield status
- intelligence score/risk
- faction-visible activity

Modules are configured in `mxf/web-dashboard.yml` and web role access is configured in `mxf/web-permissions.yml`.

title: Feature Overview
excerpt: This page summarizes what MxF Factions provides and where each system is configured. Exact syntax is in the Command Reference.
bodyMarkdown:
# Feature Overview

This page summarizes what MxF Factions provides and where each system is configured. Exact syntax is in the Command Reference.

## Core Factions

| System | What players can do | Main commands |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Factions and membership | Create/disband factions, invite/join/leave/kick, open/close joining, bans. | `/f create`, `/f invite`, `/f join`, `/f kick`, `/f disband` |
| Roles and permissions | Leader, co-leader, moderator, member/recruit roles and faction-owned action permissions. | `/f promote`, `/f demote`, `/f coleader`, `/f perms` |
| Relations and chat | Ally, truce, enemy, neutral, faction/ally chat. | `/f ally`, `/f truce`, `/f enemy`, `/f neutral`, `/f chat` |
| Territory | Chunk claims, fill/line/radius operations, ownership, maps and chunk visualization. | `/f claim`, `/f unclaim`, `/f map`, `/f seechunk`, `/f listclaims` |
| Home and warps | Faction home, named warps, passwords, configurable icons and limits. | `/f home`, `/f sethome`, `/f warp`, `/f setwarp` |
| Power and DTR | FactionsUUID power or DTR-style raidability depending on server mode. | `/f power`, `/f dtr`, `/f show` |
| Faction economy | Vault-backed faction balances and transfers. | `/f money ...` |

## Faction Operations

| System | Behavior | Configuration |
| --- | --- | --- |
| TNT bank | Persistent TNT balance, deposit, withdraw, bounded dispenser fill, siphon, audit. | `mxf/tnt.yml`, `menus/tnt.yml` |
| Native vaults/chest | Faction-owned inventory storage. PlayerVaults is not required. | `mxf/vaults.yml`, `menus/vaults.yml` |
| Roster | Optional approved-member roster and ranks. | `mxf/roster.yml`, `menus/members.yml` |
| Alts | Alt invites, limits, open/closed joining, alt entities/teleport where enabled. | `mxf/alts.yml`, `menus/members.yml` |
| Settings/permissions | GUI controls for faction-owned settings and internal access. | `menus/faction.yml`, `permissions.yml` |
| Corners | Configured premium corner locations with controlled purchase/claim/teleport behavior. | `mxf/corners.yml` |
| Live War Center | Tactical view over active raids, alerts, shield, TNT, claims, and members. | `mxf/war-center.yml`, `menus/war-center.yml` |

## Progression and Economy

| System | Behavior | Learn more |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Upgrades | YAML-defined levels, prices, requirements, and registered effects. | Custom Upgrades |
| Missions | YAML-defined objectives, filters, assignment cycles, progress, and rewards. | Custom Missions |
| Rewards | Reusable money, faction-money, Season Point, XP, item, command, and bundle actions. | Rewards |
| Shop | Configurable catalogs, multiple price providers, limits, confirmations, history, and refunds. | Shop |
| Point shop | Shop surface using Season Points where definitions are configured. | Shop |

## Competitive Systems

| System | Behavior | Learn more |
| --- | --- | --- |
| FTop/PTop | Cached faction value leaderboard; PTop reports only data supported by safe tracking. | `/f top`, `/f ptop`, `mxf/ftop.yml` |
| Season Points | Persistent non-negative faction competitive score and cached leaderboard. | Season Points |
| Season Engine | Non-destructive lifecycle, phase policy, snapshots, freeze, archive, Hall of Fame. | Season Engine |
| Season rewards | Preview-first tiers and guarded execution workflow. | Season Rewards |
| KOTH | Cuboid objective capture, contests, schedules, rewards, persistence safeguards. | KOTH |
| Outposts | Territorial control, capture modes, interval rewards, anti-farm controls, buffs. | Outposts |
| Raid Outposts | Breach, entry, core capture, rewards, cooldown, block-snapshot reset. | Raid Outposts |

## Raids and Defense

| System | Behavior | Configuration |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Grace/SOTW | Global persistent timers that can block explosions, PvP, claiming, and TNT actions. | `mxf/timers.yml` |
| Shields | Per-faction protection windows with cooldown, role control, lock/reset, and restrictions. | `mxf/shields.yml` |
| Raid sessions | Attacker/defender sessions, phases, timers, activity refresh, reservations. | `mxf/raids.yml` |
| Raid alerts | Persistent aggregated alerts from safe detection signals and personal preferences. | Raid Alerts |
| Raid recaps | Cached battle reports from tracked raid/audit/analytics data without world scans. | `mxf/raid-recaps.yml` |

## Analytics and Staff Tooling

| System | Purpose | Safety model |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Analytics | Persistent counters and snapshots from existing gameplay hooks. | No expensive command-time world scan. |
| Intelligence | Cached readiness score, strengths, weaknesses, trends, and recommendations. | Decision support, not anti-cheat evidence. |
| Audit logs | Durable faction, economy, staff, event, and system actions. | View-only operations are not spam logged. |
| Competitive rules | Definitions, cases, warnings, strikes, sanctions, evidence, notes, appeals. | Automatic detections default to alerts, not destructive punishment. |
| Investigations | Unified timeline/search/export over indexed audit activity. | Staff permissions required; private notes remain private. |
| Safe rollback | Preview, actor-bound confirmation tokens, conflict handling, history, undo. | No mutation before explicit confirmation. |

See Faction Intelligence, Investigations, and Safe Rollback.

## Web Dashboard

The web integration provides one-time in-game account linking, sessions, faction profiles, a secured API/backend bridge, and permission-aware views. Treat the web service as a separate deployment and follow Web Integration, Authentication, and Security.

## Readiness

Spigot 1.8.8 is the primary live-tested target. Complex multiplayer contests, third-party integrations, event restart edge cases, and modern server versions still require staging QA for the exact server stack. Beta-safe defaults intentionally keep destructive resets, broad automatic punishments, and unreviewed reward execution disabled.

title: Complete Feature Setup Guide
excerpt: This is the server-owner operating guide for enabling and configuring MxF Factions as a complete product. Each section identifies the feature, its files, setup sequence, commands, and minimum acceptance test.
bodyMarkdown:
# Complete Feature Setup Guide

This is the server-owner operating guide for enabling and configuring MxF Factions as a complete product. Each section identifies the feature, its files, setup sequence, commands, and minimum acceptance test.

> **Use this order:** Configure core factions and economy first, then progression, then seasons, then live events, and finally automation/staff tooling. Do not enable every schedule or reward before its providers and regions are tested.

## 1. Core Faction Rules

**Purpose:** Controls familiar FactionsUUID membership, power/DTR, claims, relations, protections, homes, faction limits, and world behavior.

**Files:** Root `config.yml` for shared MxF behavior; legacy `config/main.conf` and `config/permissions.conf` for core compatibility settings; `commands.yml`, `features.yml`, and `permissions.yml` for access.

**Setup:**

1. Choose power or DTR-style land control.
2. Set starting/max power, death loss/regeneration, claim limits/costs, faction/member limits, relation limits, and protected worlds.
3. Review wilderness, safezone, warzone and faction-territory interaction rules.
4. Review default faction actions before players create factions.
5. Create two test factions and test both authorized and denied interactions.

**Commands:** `/f create`, `/f invite`, `/f join`, `/f claim`, `/f unclaim`, `/f map`, `/f show`, `/f power`, `/f dtr`.

**Pass condition:** Membership and chunk ownership survive restart; enemies cannot bypass configured protection; overclaim/raidability follows the selected model.

## 2. Roles, Faction Permissions, Relations, and Chat

**Purpose:** Separates recruit/member/moderator/co-leader/leader authority and controls faction-owned actions.

**Files:** `permissions.yml`, legacy `config/permissions.conf`, `menus/faction.yml`, `messages/faction.yml`.

**Setup:**

1. Define what each role may do: build, containers, doors, territory, economy, TNT, home, warps, invites, kicks and promotions.
2. Keep relation/global permission selectors staff-managed.
3. Configure ally/truce/enemy limits and chat formatting.
4. Test with a real member, officer and leader account.

**Commands:** `/f promote`, `/f demote`, `/f coleader`, `/f defaultrank`, `/f perms`, `/f ally`, `/f truce`, `/f enemy`, `/f neutral`, `/f chat`.

**Pass condition:** A normal member cannot perform leader-only actions and `/f help` does not expose staff-only commands.

## 3. Homes, Warps, Corners, and Flight

**Purpose:** Provides faction travel and controlled premium territory destinations.

**Files:** `mxf/warps.yml`, `mxf/corners.yml`, `menus/warps.yml`, `messages/warps.yml`, command/core teleport settings.

**Setup:**

1. Set warp count, warmup, passwords, icon behavior and cancellation rules.
2. Define corners with world/chunk and teleport coordinates.
3. Set corner purchase cost, claim radius, minimum role, overwrite policy, confirmation and owner-only teleport.
4. Configure faction flight worlds/territories and combat cancellation.

**Commands:** `/f sethome`, `/f home`, `/f setwarp`, `/f warp`, `/f warp icon`, `/f corner list`, `/f corner claim <id>`, `/f corner teleport <id>`, `/f fly`.

**Pass condition:** Travel cancels under configured movement/combat restrictions; corners charge exactly once and use the existing claim board.

## 4. Roster and Alts

**Purpose:** Adds approved rosters and controlled faction alt membership/entities without replacing normal members.

**Files:** `mxf/roster.yml`, `mxf/alts.yml`, `menus/members.yml`, `messages/members.yml`.

**Setup:**

1. Set roster maximum, default rank and whether roster membership is required to join.
2. Set alt maximum per faction/player, invite expiry, open-join default and banned-player policy.
3. Configure alt entity spawning/teleport/removal behavior if that mode is enabled.
4. Verify disband cleanup and restart persistence.

**Commands:** `/f roster list|add|remove|rank`, `/f alt list|invite|invites|join|kick|open|close|revoke|teleport`.

**Pass condition:** Expired invites cannot be used; limits cannot be bypassed; alt heads/identity resolve safely; removal cleans the stored/entity state.

## 5. Faction Economy

**Purpose:** Provides faction balances and a shared currency source for claims, upgrades, shops, corners and rewards.

**Files:** Economy/core settings, `integrations.yml`, provider plugin configuration, relevant feature price YAML.

**Setup:**

1. Install Vault and one compatible economy provider.
2. Enable the economy hook and verify startup detects it.
3. Configure faction bank access by role.
4. Test exact deposit, withdrawal, transfer, insufficient funds and restart persistence.

**Commands:** `/f money balance`, `/f money deposit`, `/f money withdraw`, `/f money ff|fp|pf`.

**Pass condition:** Every transaction changes the expected source/destination once and appears in audit/investigation data where configured.

## 6. Native Faction Vaults and Chest

**Purpose:** Stores faction-owned inventories without PlayerVaults.

**Files:** `mxf/vaults.yml`, `menus/vaults.yml`, runtime vault data path configured by the service.

**Setup:**

1. Set inventory rows/title, default/max vault count, shared chest vault number and role access.
2. Decide whether open actions are audited.
3. Test simultaneous viewers, restart persistence, admin view and disband cleanup policy.

**Commands:** `/f chest`, `/f vault [number|menu|manager]`, `/f vaults`, staff max-vault command.

**Pass condition:** Items persist after restart and one faction cannot access another faction's vault without explicit admin permission.

## 7. TNT Bank, Fill, and Siphon

**Purpose:** Centralizes faction TNT and performs bounded nearby dispenser/dropper transactions.

**Files:** `mxf/tnt.yml`, `menus/tnt.yml`, `messages/tnt.yml`.

**Setup:**

1. Set maximum storage and deposit/withdraw-all policy.
2. Set min/max radius, maximum scanned/fill blocks, dispenser/dropper support, chunk-loading policy and territory requirement.
3. Choose distribution mode and role permissions.
4. Review Grace/SOTW/shield restrictions.

**Commands:** `/f tnt info`, `/f tnt deposit <amount|all>`, `/f tnt withdraw <amount|all>`, `/f tnt fill <radius> <amount>`, `/f tnt siphon <radius> [amount]`.

**Pass condition:** Multiple valid dispensers receive the intended total distribution, balances reconcile exactly, and repeated/rejected commands cannot duplicate TNT.

## 8. FTop and PTop

**Purpose:** Provides a cached faction value leaderboard without recalculating on every view.

**Files:** `mxf/ftop.yml`, `menus/ftop.yml`, `messages/ftop.yml`.

**Setup:**

1. Define block and spawner values, excluded worlds, page size and number format.
2. Set refresh interval, chunk budget and whether chunks may load.
3. Set manual refresh permission/cooldown.
4. Run a refresh after placing known-value blocks in claimed land.

**Commands:** `/f top [page]`, `/f top <criteria> [page]`, `/f ftop`, `/f ptop`, `/f refreshtop`.

**Pass condition:** Known values rank correctly and opening FTop repeatedly uses the cache. PTop must say unavailable rather than fabricate data when personal tracking is absent.

## 9. Missions

**Purpose:** Gives factions configurable objectives, progress cycles and rewards.

**Files:** `mxf/missions.yml`, `mxf/mission-definitions.yml`, `menus/missions.yml`, `messages/missions.yml`.

**Setup:**

1. Configure assignment mode, page size, save-on-progress and cycle behavior.
2. Define each mission's display, objective handler, target, filters and reward handlers.
3. Use only registered objective/filter/reward types shown by the admin definitions command.
4. Reload definitions and inspect diagnostics before assigning live rewards.

**Commands:** `/f missions`, `/f missions info <id>`, `/f missions admin definitions|reload`.

**Pass condition:** One supported event increments only the intended mission; completion/redeem issues the configured reward exactly once; restart preserves progress.

See Custom Missions, Objectives, Filters, and Rewards.

## 10. Upgrades

**Purpose:** Lets factions purchase configurable levels that modify supported faction systems.

**Files:** `mxf/upgrades.yml`, `mxf/upgrade-definitions.yml`, `menus/upgrades.yml`, `messages/upgrades.yml`.

**Setup:**

1. Define categories and each upgrade's display/icon.
2. Define levels with exact prices, requirements and registered effects.
3. Confirm the consuming subsystem supports every effect you enable.
4. Test max-level rejection, missing economy and insufficient funds.

**Commands:** `/f upgrades`, `/f upgrade info <id>`, `/f upgrade buy <id>`, `/f upgrades admin definitions|inspect|reload`.

**Pass condition:** Money is deducted once, the level persists, the effect changes the real subsystem, and maxed purchases cannot repeat.

See Custom Upgrades, Effects, and Requirements.

## 11. Rewards, Shop, and Point Shop

**Purpose:** Centralizes reusable reward actions and safe multi-currency purchases.

**Files:** `mxf/rewards.yml`, `mxf/reward-definitions.yml`, `mxf/shop.yml`, `mxf/shop-definitions.yml`, `menus/shop.yml`, `messages/shop.yml`.

**Setup:**

1. Create reusable reward definitions first.
2. Create shop items that reference rewards and define price, limits and confirmation.
3. Keep console-command rewards disabled unless required and reviewed.
4. Configure a Season Points shop catalog for `/f pointshop`.

**Commands:** `/f shop`, `/f shop list`, `/f shop buy ... confirm`, `/f shop history`, `/f pointshop`, `/f rewards`, staff validate/inspect/give/refund commands.

**Pass condition:** Empty catalogs show a clean empty state; configured items display; exact price and reward execute once; refund only restores supported reversible currencies.

## 12. Grace and SOTW

**Purpose:** Applies persistent server-wide protection windows.

**Files:** `mxf/timers.yml`, `messages/shields.yml` or timer-owned messages.

**Setup:** Set default duration, reminders/broadcasts and whether explosions, PvP, claiming and TNT fill are blocked for each timer.

**Commands:** `/f grace [status|start <duration>|stop]`, `/f sotw [status|start <duration>|stop]`.

**Pass condition:** Restrictions start/stop at the correct time, survive restart, reload without duplicate reminders and do not block disabled action types.

## 13. Faction Shields

**Purpose:** Provides faction-specific protection windows with cooldown and staff lock/reset controls.

**Files:** `mxf/shields.yml`, `menus/shields.yml`, `messages/shields.yml`.

**Setup:** Set duration maximum, cooldown, pending-change delay, timezone/allowed windows, minimum role and explosion/enemy/TNT restrictions.

**Commands:** `/f shield`, `/f shield set <startTime> <duration>`, `/f shield status`, `/f lockshield <faction>`, `/f resetshield <faction>`.

**Pass condition:** Active protection applies only in owned land, changes obey cooldown/lock, and state persists across restart.

## 14. Raids, Claims, Alerts, and Recaps

**Purpose:** Tracks attacker/defender raid sessions, temporary claim reservations, persistent defense alerts and post-raid reports.

**Files:** `mxf/raids.yml`, `mxf/raid-alerts.yml`, `mxf/raid-recaps.yml`, `menus/raid.yml`, `menus/raid-alerts.yml`, `menus/raid-recaps.yml`, `messages/raids.yml`.

**Setup:**

1. Configure raid duration/phases, activity refresh and maximum concurrent raids.
2. Configure reservation radius/duration/cooldown/cost/allowed worlds.
3. Tune alert detection, aggregation, severity, retention and player preferences.
4. Configure recap retention and included cached metrics.
5. Verify Grace/SOTW/shield prevention policy.

**Commands:** `/f raid`, `/f raid info`, `/f raid claim create`, `/f raid claims`, `/f raid alerts`, `/f raid history`, `/f raid recap`, staff `/f raid control`.

**Pass condition:** A qualifying signal creates/refreshes one raid, alerts deduplicate, blocked timers prevent creation, reservations expire safely, and ending a raid creates one recap.

## 15. Live War Center

**Purpose:** A tactical faction dashboard over existing raid, alert, shield, TNT, claim, FTop, intelligence and recap data.

**Files:** `mxf/war-center.yml`, `menus/war-center.yml`.

**Setup:** Enable desired sections, refresh cadence, public/disabled states and item layout. This is a view/router, not a duplicate raid engine.

**Commands:** `/f war`, `/f war menu` (aliases may include `/f warcenter`, `/f battle`, `/f battlecenter`).

**Pass condition:** Each enabled button routes to the existing subsystem and disabled/missing-data sections show an honest state.

## 16. KOTH

**Purpose:** Runs faction capture objectives inside configured cuboids.

**Files:** `mxf/koth.yml`, `mxf/koth-definitions.yml`, `menus/koth.yml`, `messages/koth.yml`.

**Setup:**

1. Create an arena.
2. Save a WorldEdit selection or manual positions.
3. Set capture/event duration, eligibility, contest behavior, reward profile and announcements.
4. Test manually before enabling a schedule.

**Commands:** `/f koth`, `/f koth status <id>`, staff `/f koth admin create|setregion|setduration|start|stop|forcecapture|enable|disable|reload`.

**Pass condition:** One faction progresses, enemies contest as configured, leaving/death/restart follows policy and reward executes once.

## 17. Outposts

**Purpose:** Persistent territorial control points with interval rewards, anti-farm cooldowns and optional buffs.

**Files:** `mxf/outposts.yml`, `mxf/outpost-definitions.yml`, `menus/outposts.yml`, `messages/outposts.yml`.

**Setup:** Define region/teleport, capture mode/time, eligibility, first reward delay, interval, minimum hold, recapture cooldown, rewards and buffs. Use manual cuboids if WorldEdit is unavailable.

**Commands:** `/f outpost`, `/f outpost info|status|top|stats|teleport`, staff `/f outpost admin create|setregion|setteleport|enable|disable|forcestart|forceend|reset|reload`.

**Pass condition:** Rewards do not pay immediately unless configured, cooldown blocks farming, owner/buffs persist and old-owner buffs are removed.

## 18. Raid Outposts

**Purpose:** Runs staged raid events with outer, breach, entry and core regions plus safe reset handling.

**Files:** `mxf/raid-outposts.yml`, `mxf/raid-outpost-definitions.yml`, `menus/raid-outposts.yml`, `messages/raid-outposts.yml`.

**Setup:** Create the definition; set outer/breach/entry/core selections and teleport; configure capture, breach, reset and reward profiles; take/test a snapshot; then manually start.

**Commands:** `/f raidoutpost`, `/f raidoutpost status|history|menu`, staff `/f raidoutpost admin ...`.

**Pass condition:** Phases advance only from valid activity, cooldown/history persist, rewards run once and block reset restores the documented supported state.

See the dedicated Raid Outpost pages for complete admin syntax and reset limitations.

## 19. Season Points, Season Engine, and Hall of Fame

**Purpose:** Tracks competitive scoring and a non-destructive season lifecycle with snapshots, frozen standings, archives and reward previews.

**Files:** `mxf/season-points.yml`, `mxf/seasons.yml`, `mxf/season-rewards.yml`, `menus/season.yml`, `messages/season.yml`.

**Setup:**

1. Set point formatting/leaderboard cache and earning hooks.
2. Configure phase durations/policies and snapshot interval.
3. Configure scoring weights and archive retention.
4. Configure reward tiers but keep preview-only during rehearsal.
5. Run a complete season on staging, including restart in every phase.

**Commands:** `/f points`, `/f pointstop`, `/f season`, `/f season top|history|rewards`, `/f hof`; staff season start/transition/snapshot/freeze/end/archive/reward controls.

**Pass condition:** Points never go negative, snapshots use cached data, frozen standings stay fixed, archive creates Hall of Fame entries and no faction data is destructively reset.

## 20. Analytics and Intelligence

**Purpose:** Tracks counters/snapshots and converts cached data into a configurable competitive readiness report.

**Files:** `mxf/analytics.yml`, `mxf/intelligence.yml`, `menus/analytics.yml`, `menus/intelligence.yml`, matching message files.

**Setup:** Enable desired tracking categories, snapshot interval/retention, intelligence cache, weights, thresholds and recommendations.

**Commands:** `/f analytics`, `/f intelligence`, `/f intelligence <faction>`, `/f intelligence guide`.

**Pass condition:** Normal gameplay updates counters, restart preserves data, command reads use cache and missing data is described as untracked rather than invented.

## 21. Audit Logs

**Purpose:** Records important faction, economy, staff, event and system actions for review.

**Files:** `mxf/audit.yml`, `menus/audit.yml`, `messages/audit.yml`.

**Setup:** Choose categories, retention, page size, visibility and whether low-value open/view actions are logged.

**Commands:** `/f logs` and its category/page/menu options.

**Pass condition:** Real mutations produce readable actor/target/action/time data; normal menu views do not flood storage; disallowed viewers cannot inspect private records.

## 22. Competitive Rules and Sanctions

**Purpose:** Defines rules, cases, warnings, strikes, evidence, notes, appeals, reversible penalties and faction sanctions.

**Files:** `mxf/competitive-rules.yml`, `mxf/rule-definitions.yml`.

**Setup:** Create rules with category/display/detection/default action/expiration; keep detection `ALERT_ONLY`; configure dedupe and safeguards; train staff before penalties are enabled.

**Commands:** `/f rules list|history|sanctions`; staff warn/strike/inspect/note/evidence/appeal/penalty/undo/detect/reload.

**Pass condition:** Detection creates one reviewable case, private notes stay private, irreversible actions remain blocked, and reversible penalties undo without deleting history.

## 23. Investigations and Safe Rollback

**Purpose:** Indexes normalized audit timelines and provides preview-first rollback of supported actions.

**Files:** `mxf/investigations.yml`, `mxf/rollback.yml`, `menus/investigations.yml`, `menus/rollback.yml`, matching messages.

**Setup:** Configure index/rebuild/export/retention, supported rollback handlers, token expiry, conflict policy and staff permissions.

**Commands:** `/f investigate ...`, `/f rollback preview|select|confirm|history|undo ...`.

**Pass condition:** Searches return real source events, exports remain private, previews do not mutate data, expired/wrong-actor tokens fail and history remains after undo.

## 24. Web Dashboard

**Purpose:** Links a Minecraft identity to the MxF web panel and exposes permission-aware faction data through a secured bridge/API.

**Files:** `mxf/web.yml`, `mxf/web-dashboard.yml`, `mxf/web-permissions.yml`, `mxf/web-theme.yml`, `mxf/web-backend.yml`, `messages/web.yml`.

**Setup:** Configure the backend endpoint/credentials in the private deployment, enable the bridge, restrict bind/network access, test time sync, then generate an in-game one-time code.

**Commands:** `/f web`, `/f panel link`, `/f web unlink|sessions|revokeall`; staff `/f web admin status|diagnose|unlink|revoke|reload`.

**Pass condition:** A fresh code links once before expiry, old codes fail, sessions can be revoked, faction authorization is enforced and secrets never appear in logs/client responses.

## 25. Menus, Messages, Commands, and Presets

**Purpose:** Lets owners reshape player UX without rebuilding.

**Files:** `menus/*.yml`, `messages/*.yml`, `commands.yml`, `features.yml`, `mxf/visuals.yml`, `presets/*/preset.yml`.

**Setup:** Change one title/slot/material/lore/sound/message/alias at a time; validate; scoped reload; reopen; test success/error/disabled/empty states. Preview/diff a preset before confirmed apply.

**Commands:** `/f config files|validate|report|inspect|reload|migrate`, `/f preset guide|list|preview|diff|apply|backups|rollback`.

**Pass condition:** YAML edits apply without rebuild, no unresolved values render, reload does not duplicate tasks/listeners and preset rollback restores the backed-up paths.

## Launch Gate

Before public launch, complete the Release and Server QA Checklist on the exact server/dependency stack. Keep beta-safe defaults in place until two-faction, economy, event, restart and season lifecycle tests pass.

title: First Player Steps
excerpt: The live `/f help` output reflects the server's enabled commands and your permissions. The examples below use standard defaults.
bodyMarkdown:
# First Player Steps

The live `/f help` output reflects the server's enabled commands and your permissions. The examples below use standard defaults.

## Create or Join

Create a faction:

```text
/f create Test
/f desc Building our first base
```

Or accept an invitation:

```text
/f join Test
```

Invite teammates:

```text
/f invite <player>
/f showinvites
```

## Secure Land

Stand in the chunk you want and use:

```text
/f claim
/f map
/f seechunk
```

Your server may require power, DTR, money, a roster entry, or a specific season phase. If claiming is denied, read the complete message and check `/f show`.

## Set Travel Points

```text
/f sethome
/f home
/f setwarp base
/f warp
```

Faction homes and warps can have warmups, passwords, role restrictions, costs, and limits.

## Build the Faction

```text
/f roster
/f promote <player>
/f perms
/f money balance
/f tnt info
/f missions
/f upgrades
/f settings
```

Faction leaders should review internal permissions before storing valuable items or granting officer roles.

## Enter Competitive Play

```text
/f top
/f points
/f season
/f koth
/f outpost
/f raid alerts
/f war
```

Use the Player and Faction Leader Guide for storage, progression, events, defense, analytics, intelligence, and discipline history.

title: GUI Configuration
excerpt: Shared MxF menu behavior is configured in `plugins/Factions/menus/shared.yml`. Feature layouts are configured in `plugins/Factions/menus/<feature>.yml`. A legacy root `menus.yml` may remain as a copy-only migration sourc
bodyMarkdown:
# GUI Configuration

Shared MxF menu behavior is configured in `plugins/Factions/menus/shared.yml`. Feature layouts are configured in `plugins/Factions/menus/<feature>.yml`. A legacy root `menus.yml` may remain as a copy-only migration source.

## Shared Menu Shape

Every generated feature menu uses the same shared presentation vocabulary. Some split files hold one menu at the root; grouped files such as `faction.yml`, `members.yml`, `season.yml`, `raid.yml`, and `vaults.yml` contain named subsections.

```yaml
menus:
  top:
title: "&6Faction Top"
size: 54
content-slots: [10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16]
items:
  entry:
    enabled: true
    material: "PAPER"
    legacy-material: "PAPER"
    legacy-data: -1
    amount: 1
    name: "&e{label}"
    lore:
      - "&7{value}"
    glow: false
    hide-flags: true
    custom-model-data: 0
    head-owner: ""
    actions:
      left-click:
        type: "SHOW_MESSAGE"
        value: ""
        permission: ""
        executor: "PLAYER"
    visibility:
      conditions: []
```

## Item Fields

- `enabled`: hides the item when false.
- `slot`: fixed inventory slot. Negative slots count backward from the end of the menu.
- `slots`: optional list used by future repeated item groups.
- `material`: preferred material name.
- `legacy-material`: legacy fallback material for old versions.
- `legacy-data`: 1.8 data value, or `-1` for none.
- `amount`: item stack count, clamped from 1 to 64.
- `name`: display name.
- `lore`: display lore.
- `glow`: adds a hidden enchant glow.
- `hide-flags`: hides enchant/attribute flags where supported.
- `custom-model-data`: modern custom model data, ignored safely on 1.8.8.
- `head-owner`: skull owner for head items.

## Actions

Generic action names:

- `NONE`
- `CLOSE`
- `BACK`
- `NEXT_PAGE`
- `PREVIOUS_PAGE`
- `REFRESH`
- `RUN_COMMAND`
- `OPEN_MENU`
- `OPEN_SUBMENU`
- `SHOW_MESSAGE`
- `CONFIRM_ACTION`

Subsystem action names are reserved for Java-backed handlers:

- `BUY_UPGRADE`
- `SET_SHIELD`
- `FILL_TNT`
- `DEPOSIT_TNT`
- `WITHDRAW_TNT`
- `TELEPORT_WARP`
- `START_KOTH`
- `STOP_KOTH`
- `CLAIM_OUTPOST`

If an action is unknown or not implemented generically, the menu falls back to its Java click handler.

## Visibility Conditions

Supported conditions:

- `HAS_FACTION`
- `NO_FACTION`
- `IS_STAFF`
- `IS_DEBUG`
- `IS_LEADER`
- `IS_OFFICER`
- `HAS_PERMISSION:<node>`
- `FEATURE_ENABLED:<feature>`
- `FEATURE_DISABLED:<feature>`

## Validation

Startup and `/f reload` warn about invalid materials, sounds, actions, slots, item amounts, update modes, animation types, visibility conditions, placeholders, menu sizes, page sizes, and duplicate slots. Validation warnings do not crash the plugin.

## Purpose-Built Layouts

Mission and TNT interfaces have purpose-built YAML fields because a generic entry cannot express their interaction safely.

- `menus.missions.category-order`, `category-slots`, `category-materials`, and `category-names` control mission navigation.
- `menus.missions.mission-slots`, `item-title`, `item-lore`, and progress-bar fields control mission cards.
- `menus.tnt.*-slot` and option-slot lists control the TNT grid.
- `menus.tnt.lore` contains separate player-facing templates for every bank action and selector.

Mission expanded-list placeholders are `{description}`, `{objectives}`, and `{rewards}`. TNT selection placeholders include `{selected_radius}`, `{selected_amount}`, `{option}`, and `{selected_status}`.

title: Support
excerpt: Use the official MxF Labs support channel or customer portal supplied with your license. Do not request MxF support from the upstream FactionsUUID project for MxF-only systems.
bodyMarkdown:
# Support

Use the official MxF Labs support channel or customer portal supplied with your license. Do not request MxF support from the upstream FactionsUUID project for MxF-only systems.

## Before Opening a Ticket

Check:

- Installation
- Configuration
- Troubleshooting
- Commands
- Permission Nodes

Include the plugin version, JAR SHA-256, server software/version, Java version, dependency versions, exact reproduction steps, relevant YAML with secrets removed, and the complete exception.

Never publish a license key, generated web secret, session token, private evidence, staff note, or unredacted runtime JSON.

## In-Game Help

Players use `/f help`. The help output is generated from the live command tree and filters entries by permissions. When menus are enabled it opens the configured help GUI; `/f help text` provides text fallback.

Server owners control command toggles/aliases in `commands.yml`, help presentation in the relevant menu YAML, and player-facing descriptions/messages through the message configuration.

## Common Questions

### Does MxF require MassiveCore?

No.

### Is `config/main.conf` the main MxF config?

It is the legacy FactionsUUID core compatibility file. Modern MxF features use `config.yml`, `features.yml`, `commands.yml`, split `menus/`, split `messages/`, and feature-owned `mxf/*.yml`. See the Configuration File Map.

### Is PlayerVaults required?

No. MxF faction vaults use native faction-owned storage.

### Should I use Bukkit `/reload`?

No. Use scoped MxF reload commands or restart the server.

### Why is a feature visible but unavailable?

Check the feature toggle, command toggle, Bukkit permission, faction role permission, season/timer policy, economy provider, optional integration, and configuration validation result.

title: Competitive Holograms
excerpt: MxF Factions supports HolographicDisplays and DecentHolograms without requiring either plugin to start. Install one compatible provider and enable its entry in `integrations.yml`.
bodyMarkdown:
# Competitive Holograms

MxF Factions supports HolographicDisplays and DecentHolograms without requiring either plugin to start. Install one compatible provider and enable its entry in `integrations.yml`.

All displays are configured in `mxf/holograms.yml`. Defaults are intentionally disabled because MxF cannot safely guess your spawn, KOTH, or Outpost coordinates. Set the real `world`, `x`, `y`, and `z`, verify the event `source-id`, then change only that display's `enabled` value to `true`.

Supported types:

- `FTOP`: cached faction value rankings, members, claims, and value movement.
- `SEASON_POINTS`: cached competitive point standings.
- `KOTH`: state, controller, progress, and remaining time for a KOTH definition id.
- `OUTPOST`: owner, state, capture progress, and reward timer for an Outpost definition id.
- `RAID_OUTPOST`: state, controller, progress, and remaining time for a Raid Outpost definition id.
- `CUSTOM`: configured static lines.

Use `/f holograms status`, `/f holograms refresh`, and `/f holograms reload` with `factions.holograms.admin`. `/f reload` also reloads the display file. MxF owns only names prefixed with `mxf_` on DecentHolograms and only handles created by this plugin on HolographicDisplays.

FTop and season holograms never invent factions. Configure `empty-line` for unoccupied ranks, for example `&7Play to claim this spot.`

title: MxF Factions Documentation
excerpt: MxF Factions is a competitive FactionsUUID fork that preserves familiar faction gameplay while adding configurable progression, events, raid tooling, seasons, analytics, staff enforcement, and a faction web panel.
bodyMarkdown:
# MxF Factions Documentation

MxF Factions is a competitive FactionsUUID fork that preserves familiar faction gameplay while adding configurable progression, events, raid tooling, seasons, analytics, staff enforcement, and a faction web panel.

This documentation is organized for three audiences:

- **Server owners** should begin with Installation, Configuration, and Integrations.
- **Players and faction leaders** should begin with the Player Guide and Commands.
- **Staff** should begin with the Staff Guide, [Competitive Rules](staff-guide.md#competitive-rules-and-sanctions), and Investigations.

> **Use staging first:** Install new builds and configuration changes on a staging server before production. Back up the complete `plugins/Factions/` folder, not only the JAR.

## Quick Start

1. Stop the server and remove conflicting Factions JARs.
2. Put `MxF-Factions.jar` in `plugins/`.
3. Start once to generate `plugins/Factions/config.yml`.
4. Set `license-key: "YOUR-LICENSE-KEY"` and restart.
5. Confirm startup reports a valid license, loaded YAML files, detected server version, and no `SEVERE` errors.
6. Run `/f config validate`, `/f compatibility`, `/f help`, and the [first-install checklist](installation.md#first-install-checklist).

## Product Areas

| Area | Included systems |
| --- | --- |
| Core factions | Factions, members, roles, relations, chat, homes, warps, claims, map, power/DTR, faction money. |
| Storage and utility | Native faction vaults/chest, TNT bank/fill/siphon, roster, alts, settings, permissions, corners. |
| Progression | Configurable missions, upgrades, reward definitions, faction shop, point shop. |
| Competition | FTop/PTop, Season Points, Season Engine, Hall of Fame, KOTH, Outposts, Raid Outposts. |
| Raid defense | Grace, SOTW, shields, raid sessions, raid claims, alerts, recaps, Live War Center. |
| Operations | Analytics, Intelligence, audit logs, competitive rules, investigations, safe rollback. |
| Presentation | YAML menus, messages, themes, sounds, compatibility adapters, PlaceholderAPI. |
| Web | In-game account linking, secured API bridge, faction dashboard, public profiles. |

See the Feature Overview for behavior, commands, configuration ownership, and readiness notes.

## Essential References

- Complete command reference
- Practical command guide
- Complete feature setup guide
- Permission nodes
- Configuration file map
- GUI configuration
- PlaceholderAPI
- Backups and runtime data
- Troubleshooting
- Compatibility

## Support Information

When reporting a problem, include the MxF version, server software/version, Java version, dependency versions, relevant YAML, reproduction steps, and the complete exception. Never publish your license key, generated web secrets, or runtime player/faction JSON.

title: Installation and First Setup
excerpt: This page takes a clean server from an empty `plugins/Factions/` folder to a playable MxF Factions installation.
bodyMarkdown:
# Installation and First Setup

This page takes a clean server from an empty `plugins/Factions/` folder to a playable MxF Factions installation.

## Requirements

| Requirement | Recommendation |
| --- | --- |
| Server | Spigot 1.8.8 is the primary live-tested target. Paper versions must use the matching compatible dependencies. |
| Java | Java 8 for 1.8.8/1.12.2; use the Java version required by modern Paper. The plugin JAR targets Java 8 bytecode. |
| License | A valid MxF Factions license for the correct product, IP, and activation identity. |
| Economy | Vault plus a compatible economy provider for money prices, faction banks, shops, and paid upgrades. |
| Permissions | LuckPerms is recommended, but Bukkit-compatible permission plugins are supported. |

Optional integrations are documented on the Integrations page.

## Fresh Installation

1. Stop the server completely.
2. Back up the server and remove every older or duplicate Factions JAR from `plugins/`.
3. Put the distributed JAR at `plugins/MxF-Factions.jar`.
4. Install the dependency JARs you plan to use. Every dependency must match the Minecraft server version.
5. Start the server once.
6. Open `plugins/Factions/config.yml` and set:

```yaml
license-key: "YOUR-LICENSE-KEY"
```

7. Restart the server. License validation happens before faction gameplay services load.
8. Read the complete startup block. Confirm the detected Minecraft and Java versions, loaded YAML files, storage paths, integrations, and license result.
9. Join the server and run:

```text
/f version
/f compatibility
/f config status
/f config validate
/f help
```

10. Complete the checklist below before allowing normal players to join.

> **Do not use Bukkit /reload:** Use MxF's scoped `/f reload` and `/f config reload ...` commands. For JAR, dependency, license, or major integration changes, perform a full server restart.

## What Generates

The current build manages:

- Root controls such as `config.yml`, `commands.yml`, `features.yml`, `permissions.yml`, `integrations.yml`, and `diagnostics.yml`.
- Feature menus under `menus/*.yml`.
- Feature messages under `messages/*.yml`.
- Gameplay settings and definitions under `mxf/*.yml`.
- Runtime state under `data/*.json`.
- Compatibility `.conf` files retained from FactionsUUID where needed.
- Preset definitions under `presets/<preset>/preset.yml`.

See Configuration Files for ownership and reload rules.

## Playable Baseline Setup

The generated definitions provide a staging baseline, but every server economy is different. Before launch:

1. Configure Vault and verify `/f money balance`.
2. Review claim costs, power/DTR mode, faction limits, relation limits, and protection behavior.
3. Review TNT storage/fill limits in `mxf/tnt.yml`.
4. Review upgrade prices and effects in `mxf/upgrade-definitions.yml`.
5. Review mission targets and rewards in `mxf/mission-definitions.yml`.
6. Review shops and reusable rewards in `mxf/shop-definitions.yml` and `mxf/reward-definitions.yml`.
7. Leave schedules disabled until KOTH, Outpost, and Raid Outpost regions are configured and tested.
8. Keep season rewards preview-only until the complete season lifecycle has been rehearsed.
9. Keep competitive rule detections alert-only until staff review false positives.
10. Grant staff permissions deliberately; do not hand out `factions.*` to normal ranks.

## First-Install Checklist

- [ ] License reports valid and no key appears in logs.
- [ ] No `InvalidPluginException`, `UnsupportedClassVersionError`, or startup `SEVERE` appears.
- [ ] `/f config validate` reports no blocking YAML errors.
- [ ] `/f compatibility` detects the correct server family.
- [ ] Create two test factions and invite one player into each.
- [ ] Claim, unclaim, map, set home, set warp, and restart the server.
- [ ] Verify faction, player, claim, home, and warp persistence.
- [ ] Deposit/withdraw faction money and TNT with exact balance checks.
- [ ] Open `/f help`, `/f settings`, `/f perms`, `/f missions`, `/f upgrades`, `/f shop`, `/f tnt menu`, and `/f war`.
- [ ] Confirm no menu contains `null`, `N/A`, `MemorySection`, raw enum names, internal IDs, or unresolved `{placeholders}`.
- [ ] Run `/f saveall`, then stop the server normally and confirm clean shutdown.

## Existing Installation

Do not place a new JAR directly on production without a migration rehearsal.

1. Clone the server or copy `plugins/Factions/` into staging.
2. Back up and hash YAML and JSON before the upgrade.
3. Replace only the JAR. Keep the existing data folder.
4. Start and review migration/validation output.
5. Confirm custom keys remain and missing defaults are appended.
6. Compare faction counts, player counts, board claims, balances, season data, missions, upgrades, and event state.
7. Run the full smoke checklist.

The full process is on Migration and Upgrades.

title: Integrations and Dependencies
excerpt: MxF Factions declares integrations as soft dependencies. The core plugin should start without optional plugins, while the affected feature reports that its provider is unavailable.
bodyMarkdown:
# Integrations and Dependencies

MxF Factions declares integrations as soft dependencies. The core plugin should start without optional plugins, while the affected feature reports that its provider is unavailable.

## Recommended Stack

| Plugin | Purpose | Required? |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Vault | Common economy and permission service bridge. | Strongly recommended. |
| Economy provider | Personal/faction money, paid upgrades, shops and rewards. | Required for configured money actions. |
| LuckPerms | Bukkit permissions and optional contextual permission support. | Recommended. |
| PlaceholderAPI | `%mxf_*%` and `%factionsuuid_*%` placeholders. | Optional. |
| WorldEdit | Region selection for KOTH, Outposts and Raid Outposts. | Optional; manual definitions exist. |
| WorldGuard | Region compatibility hooks. | Optional. |
| Essentials/EssentialsX | Common economy/teleport/chat ecosystem integration. | Optional. |
| dynmap | Legacy faction map integration. | Optional. |

PlayerVaults is not required. MxF faction vaults use native faction-owned persistence.

## Version Matching

Use a dependency release compatible with the exact Minecraft server version. A modern WorldEdit or PlaceholderAPI JAR is not automatically safe on Spigot 1.8.8.

After changing dependency JARs:

1. Stop the server.
2. Replace the dependency.
3. Start normally.
4. Run `/f compatibility` and `/f compatibility report` as staff.
5. Exercise the actual integration path, not only startup.

## Vault and Economy

Without a working Vault economy provider:

- Money prices and payouts fail cleanly.
- Faction money commands may be unavailable.
- Paid upgrades and shop items using money cannot complete.
- Non-money currencies and systems can continue when configured.

Test an exact deposit, exact withdrawal, insufficient-funds denial, shop purchase, upgrade purchase, refund, restart, and audit entry.

## PlaceholderAPI

When installed, MxF registers its bundled expansions during startup. Use `/papi parse <player> <placeholder>` to test values and review PlaceholderAPI.

Internal `{placeholder}` values used by MxF menus/messages do not require PlaceholderAPI.

## WorldEdit and WorldGuard

WorldEdit can capture a player's selection for event regions. WorldGuard support is adapter-based and depends on installed API version. Always test:

- Missing-plugin fallback.
- Region creation/editing.
- Reload after region edits.
- Capture entry/exit and boundary coordinates.
- Shutdown/restart during an event.

## Web Dashboard

The dashboard uses the MxF web configuration and backend bridge, not PlaceholderAPI. Keep API credentials, generated secrets, link/session data, and backend endpoints private. Follow Web Integration and Web Security.

title: Faction Intelligence Guide
excerpt: Faction Intelligence turns already tracked faction data into a cached competitive readiness report. It does not scan chunks, inspect containers, infer a raid winner, or invent missing values when a player opens the menu.
bodyMarkdown:
# Faction Intelligence Guide

Faction Intelligence turns already tracked faction data into a cached competitive readiness report. It does not scan chunks, inspect containers, infer a raid winner, or invent missing values when a player opens the menu.

## Commands

- `/f intelligence` - view your faction report.
- `/f intelligence <faction>` - view another faction with `factions.intelligence.others` or admin access.
- `/f intelligence menu [faction]` - open the configured GUI.
- `/f intelligence guide` - show the live cache, weights, and risk thresholds from YAML.
- `/f intel` and `/f report` - aliases.

## Score

Each component produces a value from 0 to 100. The overall score is the weighted average:

`sum(component score * configured weight) / sum(configured weights)`

Default weights in `mxf/intelligence.yml` are:

| Component | Weight | Main data used |
| --- | ---: | --- |
| Activity | 20 | Analytics update age and recent audit events |
| Members | 15 | Members, joins, leaves, roster coverage, and alt ratio |
| TNT | 15 | Current TNT bank/capacity and tracked deposit/use flow |
| Raids | 20 | Active raids, raid claims, breaches, hostile entries, and tracked losses |
| Shields | 10 | Active shield state and tracked shield changes |
| Upgrades | 10 | Purchased levels compared with enabled configured levels |
| FTop | 10 | Latest cached FTop value and snapshot growth direction |

Weights may be changed without rebuilding the plugin. A component with incomplete source data uses its documented conservative baseline; the report then says that more tracked data is needed instead of presenting fabricated precision.

## Risk Levels

Risk describes competitive readiness, not a punishment or guaranteed raid outcome.

- `LOW`: score at or above `thresholds.low` (default 75).
- `MEDIUM`: score at or above `thresholds.medium` (default 50).
- `HIGH`: score at or above `thresholds.high` (default 25).
- `CRITICAL`: score below the high threshold.

Higher score means lower assessed risk. Server owners can change every threshold in `mxf/intelligence.yml`.

## Component Behavior

### Activity

Recent analytics updates establish the baseline. Recent audit activity can add a bounded bonus. Old or absent activity gradually lowers this component.

### Members

Member count, join/leave stability, roster coverage, and an unusually high alt-to-member ratio affect the result. It does not inspect player behavior outside data already tracked by MxF.

### TNT

The current faction TNT bank is compared with the effective configured capacity, including upgrade bonuses. Deposits and siphons help indicate reserves; fills and withdrawals can indicate pressure when the remaining bank is low.

### Raids

Tracked raid starts and claims provide activity context. Active hostile pressure, confirmed breaches, hostile entries, and tracked spawner losses reduce readiness. No world scan is performed.

### Shields

An active shield and tracked shield management improve the component. A missing shield can produce a recommendation when enabled in YAML.

### Upgrades

Purchased upgrade levels are compared with the total levels of currently enabled upgrade definitions. Disabled definitions are not counted.

### FTop

The latest cached FTop value and movement between analytics snapshots are used. Opening the report never triggers an FTop world recalculation.

## Strengths, Weaknesses, And Recommendations

Strengths and weaknesses are threshold-based explanations of component scores. Recommendations are optional rules controlled under `recommendations` in `mxf/intelligence.yml`, including low TNT, inactive members, weak upgrades, missing shields, and raid risk.

The Recent Trends section summarizes cached members, roster/alts, claims, TNT flow, raids, upgrades, money flow, FTop, and recent audit activity when those sources exist.

## Cache And Persistence

- Reports are cached for `cache-seconds` (default 300).
- GUI/command reads normally use the cached report.
- Refresh regenerates from cached services, not from chunk scans.
- Cache data is stored in `data/intelligence_reports.json`.
- Invalid JSON is quarantined rather than silently overwriting the damaged file.
- Data saves during autosave, `/f saveall`, and normal shutdown.

## Configuration Ownership

- `mxf/intelligence.yml`: enablement, cache, weights, thresholds, recommendations, and `/f intelligence guide` text.
- `menus/intelligence.yml`: title, size, item slots, materials, names, lore, guide item, refresh item, and menu-specific effects.
- `features.yml`: global intelligence feature toggle.
- `commands.yml`: command toggle, aliases, permission overlay, descriptions, usage, and cooldown.
- `messages/intelligence.yml` or the active message overlay: player-facing command messages.

## Interpretation

Use Intelligence as a decision-support view. It is strongest after analytics snapshots and normal server activity have accumulated. It should not be treated as anti-cheat evidence, a guaranteed raid forecast, or an automatic disciplinary system.

title: Introduction to Factions
excerpt: It's a group of players, like a team or a club. A faction can **claim territory**, work together to **build a base**,
bodyMarkdown:
# Introduction to Factions

## What is a faction?

It's a group of players, like a team or a club. A faction can **claim territory**, work together to **build a base**,
and build (or destroy) relationships with other factions. 

## Territory

> **Land control in Factions is per chunk:** In Minecraft, a "chunk" is a 3D piece of the Minecraft world. It goes from the bedrock all the way into the sky (y) and is 16 blocks by 16 blocks in the horizontal dimensions (x and z).

A faction claims land by chunk, dependent on how much [power](#power) they possess at a rate of 1 chunk per power or at
a set amount per faction member when in [DTR](#dtr) mode.
Within its territory, members of the faction can exclusively build without interference of non-members (configurable
through the `/f perms` command). Your faction's territory is where you can build your base, set your faction's home 
location, create warp points, and more.

Claiming territory is performed with the `/f claim` command.

> **Note:** Want to see the boundaries of the chunk in which you're standing? Use the `/f seechunk` command!

## Power

Every player has a quantity of "power." A new player typically starts with 0 power and slowly gains power over time by
playing on the server. Dying takes a way a set amount of power. 

When a player is part of a faction, their faction's power is calculated as a total of its players' power. Power is how
factions acquire territory, or raid another faction. A faction can claim territory as long as its power is greater than
its current territory claims.

If, through player death or players leaving a faction, territory count is greater than power the faction can be raided
by enemies who can now destroy blocks in their enemy's territory and maybe even claim territory away from the faction.

## DTR

An alternative to power, Deaths 'til Raidable is a mode in which land claiming does not depend on power but instead
factions can claim a certain amount based on faction membership. Every time a faction member dies, the faction's DTR
value decreases (typically by 1) and once it hits 0 the faction's land can be raided and looted.

bodyMarkdown:
# MxF KOTH

MxF KOTH adds configurable King of the Hill arenas using the existing FactionsUUID faction model and MxF YAML architecture.

## Files

- `plugins/Factions/mxf/koth.yml`: engine, rewards, schedules, messages, and audit settings.
- `plugins/Factions/mxf/koth-definitions.yml`: arena definitions.
- `plugins/Factions/menus/koth.yml`: KOTH GUI layout.
- `plugins/Factions/commands.yml`: `/f koth` command toggle and aliases.
- `plugins/Factions/features.yml`: global `koth` toggle.

## Commands

Command | Permission | Description
--- | --- | ---
`/f koth` | `factions.koth` | Show active KOTH events
`/f koth list` | `factions.koth.list` | List configured arenas
`/f koth status [id]` | `factions.koth.status` | Show arena status
`/f koth menu` | `factions.koth` | Open the KOTH GUI
`/f koth admin create <id>` | `factions.koth.admin.create` | Create an arena from WorldEdit selection or a fallback cuboid around the player
`/f koth admin delete <id>` | `factions.koth.admin.delete` | Delete an arena
`/f koth admin setregion <id>` | `factions.koth.admin.setregion` | Import a WorldEdit selection
`/f koth admin setpos1 <id>` | `factions.koth.admin.setregion` | Set manual cuboid position 1
`/f koth admin setpos2 <id>` | `factions.koth.admin.setregion` | Set manual cuboid position 2
`/f koth admin start <id>` | `factions.koth.admin.start` | Start an event
`/f koth admin stop <id>` | `factions.koth.admin.stop` | Stop an event
`/f koth admin forcecapture <id> <faction>` | `factions.koth.admin.forcecapture` | Force a faction capture
`/f koth admin setduration <id> <seconds>` | `factions.koth.admin.setduration` | Set capture duration
`/f koth admin enable <id>` | `factions.koth.admin.enable` | Enable an arena
`/f koth admin disable <id>` | `factions.koth.admin.disable` | Disable an arena
`/f koth admin reload` | `factions.koth.admin.reload` | Reload KOTH YAML
`/f koth admin save` | `factions.koth.admin.save` | Save arena definitions

## Capture Behavior

- Only normal player factions can capture.
- The configured minimum number of faction members must be inside the cuboid.
- If one eligible faction is inside, capture progress advances.
- If multiple eligible factions are inside, the event becomes contested.
- If no eligible faction is inside, the event becomes empty.
- Progress loss while contested or empty is controlled by `progress-loss-mode`: `PAUSE`, `RESET`, or `DECAY`.

## Rewards

Reward profiles in `mxf/koth.yml` can issue:

- Season Points
- faction bank money
- console commands

Reward commands support `%faction%`, `%faction_id%`, `%koth%`, `%koth_name%`, `%leader%`, `%season_points%`, and matching brace placeholders.

## Scheduling

Schedules are configured in `mxf/koth.yml -> schedules`.

Each schedule includes:

- `enabled`
- `koth`
- `days`
- `time`

The scheduler prevents the same schedule from firing more than once in the same minute.

## Persistence

Arena definitions persist in `mxf/koth-definitions.yml`. Existing `mxf/koths.yml` values are copied safely during migration and the legacy file is preserved.

Active event sessions are not persisted. A running KOTH is cancelled on shutdown/restart so rewards are not duplicated and stale capture state does not survive a crash.

## Beta Feedback Fixes

- `/f koth` opens the overview GUI for players when menus are enabled.
- `/f koth text` and `/f koth overview` provide explicit text fallback.
- Capture state now distinguishes first capture, abandonment, same-faction resume, contested state, contest end, and different-faction takeover.
- Resume and takeover messages are configurable through the KOTH message config.
- Menu rendering now passes through the shared safe placeholder renderer to avoid leaking raw placeholders.

Physical winner-only reward chests are deferred to the shared capture reward claim service documented in `docs/capture-reward-claims.md`.

title: Runtime Licensing
excerpt: MxF Factions checks its MxF Labs license before loading factions, players, claims, commands, listeners, or gameplay services.
bodyMarkdown:
# Runtime Licensing

MxF Factions checks its MxF Labs license before loading factions, players, claims, commands, listeners, or gameplay services.

## Setup

1. Install the latest `MxF-Factions.jar`.
2. Start the server once.
3. Open `plugins/Factions/config.yml`.
4. Set the single customer-editable license value:

```yml
license-key: "MXF-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX"
```

5. Restart the server.
6. Confirm the console reports `MxF license valid` before faction data loads.

The API URL, product slug, request timeouts, device identity, install identity, and enforcement rules are internal and are not configurable by resource owners.

## Startup Decisions

The plugin starts only when the live activation response is valid for MxF Factions. It fails closed for:

- missing or invalid keys
- wrong product
- expired, revoked, suspended, or blacklisted licenses
- HWID/device mismatch
- strict IP mismatch
- activation limit reached
- blocked plugin version
- suspicious account activity
- malformed API responses
- an unavailable license API

The API denial code and safe message are printed to console. The full license key is never logged.

## Installation Identity

`plugins/Factions/mxf-license-instance.id` is generated automatically and is not configuration. It gives the server installation a stable identity for activation and HWID checks.

Moving the installation to another machine may require an activation reset through MxF Labs. Do not copy one plugin data folder to several active servers.

## Security

- Keep `config.yml` private because it contains the customer key.
- Do not paste the full key into public tickets, screenshots, or logs.
- The API endpoint uses HTTPS and is pinned in the plugin.
- Product and version policy is decided by the MxF Labs license record.
- A key change takes effect on the next full server restart, before gameplay initialization.

title: Migration and Upgrades
excerpt: MxF Factions extends FactionsUUID storage and adds YAML/JSON services. A safe migration keeps the JAR and its matching data folder together.
bodyMarkdown:
# Migration and Upgrades

MxF Factions extends FactionsUUID storage and adds YAML/JSON services. A safe migration keeps the JAR and its matching data folder together.

## Before Upgrading

1. Stop the server cleanly.
2. Copy the complete `plugins/Factions/` folder outside the server directory.
3. Back up worlds, economy, permissions, and relevant integration data.
4. Record the old plugin version and JAR SHA-256.
5. Record hashes of YAML and JSON so unexpected changes are visible.

Windows hash example:

```powershell
Get-FileHash .\plugins\MxF-Factions.jar -Algorithm SHA256
Get-ChildItem .\plugins\Factions -Recurse -File |
  Get-FileHash -Algorithm SHA256 |
  Export-Csv .\mxf-before-upgrade.csv -NoTypeInformation
```

## Staging Migration

1. Copy production data to an isolated staging server.
2. Replace only the plugin JAR.
3. Keep the valid license/install identity appropriate for that server.
4. Start once and inspect every migration, YAML, definition, integration, and storage message.
5. Run `/f config status` and `/f config validate`.
6. Confirm the plugin did not replace customized values with defaults.
7. Confirm missing files/keys were added without deleting legacy compatibility data.
8. Compare the before/after hash manifests and inspect every changed file.
9. Test save, reload, shutdown, and restart persistence.

## Legacy Configuration

Legacy FactionsUUID `.conf` files can remain for compatibility. Current MxF settings belong in root YAML, split `menus/`, split `messages/`, and `mxf/*.yml`. Do not delete legacy files until the current build reports they are no longer a migration or fallback source.

The commonly encountered `config/main.conf` is a legacy compatibility file. It is not the single source for modern MxF features. Start with `config.yml` and use the Configuration File Map to find the owning feature file.

## Rollback

If migration fails:

1. Stop the server immediately.
2. Preserve the failed folder and logs for diagnosis.
3. Restore the old JAR.
4. Restore the complete matching `plugins/Factions/` backup.
5. Restore external economy/permissions data if the failed test changed it.
6. Start and verify version, faction count, board claims, balances, and clean shutdown.

Never restore only `factions.json` while leaving newer related data stores in place unless support specifically directs it.

title: MxF Outposts

bodyMarkdown:
# MxF Outposts

MxF Outposts are internal territorial control points for competitive factions servers. They work like KoTH/control-point objectives: admins define a region, factions stand inside it to capture, enemy factions contest, and the winning faction owns the outpost until another faction captures it.

Outposts do not create FactionsUUID claims by default and do not require a special outpost faction. WorldGuard and WorldEdit are optional helpers only; ownership lives in `OutpostService` and persists to `plugins/Factions/data/outposts.json`.

## Commands

Command | Permission | Description
--- | --- | ---
`/f outpost` | `factions.outpost` | Opens the outpost menu or text fallback
`/f outpost list` | `factions.outpost` | Lists configured outposts
`/f outpost info <id>` | `factions.outpost` | Shows detailed status and reward data
`/f outpost status <id>` | `factions.outpost` | Shows compact status
`/f outpost top` | `factions.outpost.stats` | Shows faction outpost leaderboard
`/f outpost stats [faction]` | `factions.outpost.stats` | Shows faction outpost stats
`/f outpost teleport <id>` | `factions.outpost.teleport` | Teleports to the configured outpost point
`/f outpost admin` | `factions.outpost.admin` | Opens the admin menu or admin usage
`/f outpost admin create <id>` | `factions.outpost.admin` | Creates a disabled manual-cuboid definition
`/f outpost admin delete <id>` | `factions.outpost.admin` | Deletes a definition from YAML and runtime state
`/f outpost admin setregion <id>` | `factions.outpost.admin` | Imports WorldEdit selection or creates a manual fallback cuboid
`/f outpost admin setteleport <id>` | `factions.outpost.admin` | Sets the teleport point to your location
`/f outpost admin enable <id>` | `factions.outpost.admin` | Enables an outpost
`/f outpost admin disable <id>` | `factions.outpost.admin` | Disables an outpost
`/f outpost admin forcestart <id>` | `factions.outpost.admin` | Activates an outpost
`/f outpost admin forceend <id>` | `factions.outpost.admin` | Deactivates an outpost
`/f outpost admin reset <id>` | `factions.outpost.admin` | Clears owner/progress/stats state
`/f outpost admin reload` | `factions.outpost.admin` | Reloads outpost YAML and restarts the tick task

Aliases: `/f outposts`, `/f opost`, `/f controlpoint`, `/f controlpoints`.

## Capture Modes

- `STAND`: one uncontested faction inside the region progresses capture.
- `KING_OF_THE_HILL`: same safe MVP behavior as `STAND`, separated for config compatibility.
- `DOMINATION`: the faction with the most players inside progresses capture only when it has a unique lead.
- `PAYLOAD`, `SCORE`, and `OBJECTIVE`: parsed as future placeholders and treated as not implemented.

## Regions

- `MANUAL`: configured cuboid coordinates in `mxf/outpost-definitions.yml` (or migrated legacy definitions retained by the current installation).
- `WORLDGUARD`: optional region id lookup when WorldGuard is installed. Manual coordinates remain a fallback.
- `WorldEdit`: optional admin setup helper for `/f outpost admin setregion <id>`.

## Rewards And Buffs

Outpost rewards are interval-based. Capturing does not immediately farm rewards unless config later enables that behavior.

Configurable reward outputs include faction bank money, faction TNT bank, online member money, console commands, player commands, and internal outpost points. Anti-abuse settings include minimum hold time, full-interval first reward delay, per-faction/outpost cooldown, and global reward cooldown.

Buffs use Bukkit potion effects for eligible faction members while their faction owns an active outpost. Buffs are cleaned up when ownership changes, the outpost disables, the player leaves eligibility, or the service shuts down.

## YAML

Primary config:

- `plugins/Factions/mxf/outposts.yml`

Menu config:

- `plugins/Factions/menus/outposts.yml` (`outposts`, `detail`, and `admin` sections)

Persistence:

- `plugins/Factions/data/outposts.json`

## Placeholders

PlaceholderAPI examples:

- `%mxf_outpost_owner_<id>%`
- `%mxf_outpost_status_<id>%`
- `%mxf_outpost_progress_<id>%`
- `%mxf_outpost_contested_<id>%`
- `%mxf_outpost_players_<id>%`
- `%mxf_outpost_timeheld_<id>%`
- `%mxf_outpost_nextreward_<id>%`
- `%mxf_outposts_owned_<faction>%`
- `%mxf_outposts_points_<faction>%`
- `%mxf_outposts_top_1%`

MxF menu/message placeholders include `{outpost}`, `{outpost_id}`, `{outpost_owner}`, `{outpost_status}`, `{outpost_progress}`, `{outpost_players}`, `{outpost_mode}`, `{outpost_reward}`, `{outposts_owned}`, `{outpost_time_held}`, `{outpost_next_reward}`, and `{outpost_contested}`.

## Live QA Notes

Verified on the Windows 1.8.8 dev server with Vault, EssentialsX, PlaceholderAPI, WorldEdit, WorldGuard, and LuckPerms installed:

- Startup generated `mxf/outposts.yml` and `data/outposts.json`, loaded one active manual-cuboid outpost, and completed MxF YAML validation with no warnings.
- `/f outpost list`, `/f outpost info central`, `/f outpost status central`, `/f outpost top`, and `/f outpost admin reload` ran from console without severe errors.
- `/f reload` reloaded outpost YAML and restarted the outpost runtime task once.
- `/f outpost admin forceend central` and `/f outpost admin forcestart central` correctly toggled active state after command argument metadata was hardened.
- `/f outpost admin setregion <id>` and `/f outpost admin setteleport <id>` are intentionally player-only because they require a player location or WorldEdit selection.
- PlaceholderAPI detected and registered the `mxf` expansion; console parsing still requires an online player target in the tested PlaceholderAPI build.
- Shutdown saved outposts and removed runtime buffs/tasks cleanly.

Still test in-game with two factions before calling outposts production-ready: actual region capture, contested enemy capture, menu clicks, setregion with a WorldEdit selection, interval rewards after the first full delay, recapture cooldowns, and potion buff application/removal on real online faction members.

## Deferred

- Bossbar/actionbar/title announcement delivery waits for the multi-version message adapter.
- Full PAYLOAD/SCORE/OBJECTIVE capture engines are future work.
- Web dashboard export is future work.
- Physical owner-only reward claim chests are deferred to the shared capture reward claim service documented in `docs/capture-reward-claims.md`.

## Beta Feedback Fixes

- Capture abandonment is now tracked and announced instead of silently decaying.
- Same-faction resume and controller-change announcements are configurable.
- Teleport access can be configured with availability modes: `DISABLED`, `ANYONE_WHILE_ACTIVE`, `PARTICIPANTS_WHILE_ACTIVE`, `OWNER_ONLY`, `OWNER_AND_ALLIES`, and `STAFF_ONLY`.
- Teleport can require a fully captured state before it appears or succeeds.
- Outpost overview/detail menus use safe placeholder rendering and friendly state labels.

## MxF Polish Notes

The premium configuration, placeholder, YAML validation, generated help, and shared menu theme systems mostly use the existing command permission checks listed above. Player UX staff/debug visibility adds the small nodes listed below.

Server owners can still customize displayed permission strings and faction-permission menu labels through `plugins/Factions/permissions.yml`. Bukkit permission enforcement remains in `plugin.yml` and Java.

## MxF Player UX

| Node |
|------|
| factions.ui.staffdetails |
| factions.ui.debug |

`factions.ui.staffdetails` reveals exact timestamps, internal ids, scan/calculation details, and metadata in supported menus.

`factions.ui.debug` is reserved for owner/developer diagnostics and bypasses normal player lore trimming.
## Season Engine

- `factions.season` - view the season engine and timeline.
- `factions.season.top` - view season leaderboard standings.
- `factions.season.history` - view archived season history.
- `factions.season.admin` - start/end/archive seasons, inspect engine state, freeze standings, and create snapshots.
- `factions.season.force` - force phase transitions after explicit confirmation.
- `factions.season.rewards` - view reward tiers and previews.
- `factions.season.rewards.admin` - confirm or execute reward hooks when beta safety allows it.

## Competitive Rules

- `factions.rules` - access competitive rules commands.
- `factions.rules.view` - view own faction public discipline history.
- `factions.rules.staff` - inspect cases, create warnings/strikes, and view staff-only notes/evidence.
- `factions.rules.admin` - administer competitive enforcement state.
- `factions.rules.detect` - run configured automatic detections manually.
- `factions.rules.appeal` - update appeal/upheld/overturned/expired states.
- `factions.rules.penalty` - manually apply configured penalties.
- `factions.rules.undo` - reverse reversible disciplinary penalties.

## Raid Alerts

- `factions.raid.alerts` - view raid alerts.
- `factions.raid.alerts.history` - view raid alert history and detail output.
- `factions.raid.alerts.settings` - manage personal raid alert preferences.
- `factions.raid.alerts.acknowledge` - acknowledge faction raid alerts.
- `factions.raid.alerts.admin` - general raid alert administration.
- `factions.raid.alerts.admin.test` - create safe test alerts.
- `factions.raid.alerts.admin.inspect` - inspect raid alert snapshots and other factions' alerts.
- `factions.raid.alerts.admin.reload` - reload raid alert YAML.

## Raid Outposts

- `factions.raidoutpost` - access Raid Outpost commands.
- `factions.raidoutpost.list` - list configured Raid Outposts.
- `factions.raidoutpost.status` - view Raid Outpost status.
- `factions.raidoutpost.history` - view Raid Outpost history.
- `factions.raidoutpost.admin` - access Raid Outpost admin commands.
- `factions.raidoutpost.admin.create` - create Raid Outpost definitions.
- `factions.raidoutpost.admin.delete` - delete Raid Outpost definitions.
- `factions.raidoutpost.admin.setregion` - assign regions and teleport points.
- `factions.raidoutpost.admin.start` - manually start events.
- `factions.raidoutpost.admin.stop` - stop events.
- `factions.raidoutpost.admin.forcecapture` - force a capture through the normal completion path.
- `factions.raidoutpost.admin.reset` - run configured resets.
- `factions.raidoutpost.admin.snapshot` - capture reset snapshots.
- `factions.raidoutpost.admin.setduration` - change event duration.
- `factions.raidoutpost.admin.enable` - enable definitions.
- `factions.raidoutpost.admin.disable` - disable definitions.
- `factions.raidoutpost.admin.inspect` - inspect staff diagnostics.
- `factions.raidoutpost.admin.reload` - reload Raid Outpost YAML.
- `factions.raidoutpost.admin.save` - save runtime state.

## Investigation And Rollback

- `factions.investigate` - access investigation commands.
- `factions.investigate.faction` - inspect faction timelines.
- `factions.investigate.player` - inspect player timelines.
- `factions.investigate.transactions` - inspect transaction views.
- `factions.investigate.events` - inspect event details and related events.
- `factions.investigate.cases` - manage staff investigation cases.
- `factions.investigate.export` - export local reports.
- `factions.investigate.rebuild` - rebuild/reload the index.
- `factions.investigate.debug` - reserved for detailed diagnostics.
- `factions.rollback` - access rollback commands.
- `factions.rollback.preview` - preview rollback operations.
- `factions.rollback.execute` - confirm rollback execution.

- `factions.rollback.exact` - reserved for configured exact restores.

- `factions.rollback.history` - view rollback records.

Rollback nodes are not granted to the default player kit.

## Reward Economy And Shop

- `factions.shop` - open configurable MxF shops.
- `factions.shop.buy` - purchase configured shop items.
- `factions.shop.history` - view relevant purchase history.

## Web Dashboard

## Configuration

## Competitive Interfaces And Integrations
