title: /f perms
excerpt: You can control who, inside or outside your faction, has access to various actions within your territory.
bodyMarkdown:
# /f perms

> **Looking for permission nodes? [Click here](permissionnodes.md):**

You can control who, inside or outside your faction, has access to various actions within your territory.

## How it all works: Selectors and Actions

Determination of who can perform what actions is sorted out by *selectors*, which then have defined for them a series of 
allowed or denied *actions*. For example, a selector may describe all members of the faction with at least the 
moderator role, and that selector is allowed the `setwarp` action, so they may set warps within the faction. Or, perhaps 
a selector describing allies of the faction is allowed to open doors within the faction, as this faction wants their 
allies to enter their territory. 

To determine if a player can perform an action, the selectors for a given faction are checked *in order*. If a player 
matches a selector, the matched selector is then checked for the action. If the action is found then that state, 
allow or deny, is used. Only the first selector-matched action state (allow/deny) is used. For anyone familiar with 
Discord permissions, this system behaves similarly. If no selectors match, or the action is not present in any matching 
selectors, the action is denied.

Server admins can control *override* permissions, defining selectors and actions that will apply to *all factions* and 
will be processed *before* any faction-set selectors are checked. As an example usage, a server admin could *force* all 
factions to allow allies to use buttons through an ally matching override selector with `button` set to allow. In the 
config, admins can additionally set actions to *hidden* and this will prevent the action from showing up anywhere. It 
is not advisable to hide an action after the server has been running without also setting an `all` override selector for 
it to avoid any previously set options from being unchangeable by faction admins.

All of this functionality can be controlled by a faction leader through the `/f perms` command, just by running that 
base command and clicking the text within to add/remove/reorder selectors.

## Actions

Action | Description
--- | --- 
ban | Can ban others from the faction
build | Can build in faction territory (while not raidable)
button | Use buttons in faction territory (while not raidable)
container | Use containers in faction territory (while not raidable)
destroy | Can destroy in faction territory (while not raidable)
disband | Can disband the faction (careful!)
door | Use doors in faction territory (while not raidable)
economy | Can access faction economy
fly | Can fly in faction territory
frost | Can frost walk in faction territory (while not raidable)
home | Can visit the faction home
invite | Able to invite others to the faction
item | Use items in faction territory (while not raidable)
kick | Can kick faction members
lever | Use levers in faction territory (while not raidable)
owner | Can created owned areas with /f owner
pain | Allows building/destroying in faction territory but causes pain (while not raidable)
plate | Can interact with pressure plates
promote | Can promote members up to their own role within the faction
sethome | Can set the faction home
setwarp | Can set a faction warp
territory | Can claim/unclaim faction territory
tntdeposit | Can deposit into faction TNT bank (including siphon)
tntwithdraw | Can withdraw from faction TNT bank (including fill)
warp | Can use faction warps

## Selectors

> **Any selector that can match a faction (all, faction, relation) can be used for non-player actions like pistons:**

Selector | Description
--- | ---
all | Matches everything, always. 
faction | Matches a specific faction. Can input the faction name, but will be stored using non-changing internal ID.
player | Matches a specific player. Can input the player name, but will be stored using non-changing Mojang UUID.
relation-single | Matches a specific relation. 
relation-atleast | Matches a relation of at least the given type. Order: Enemy, Neutral, Truce, Ally.
relation-atmost | Matches a relation of at most the given type. Order: Enemy, Neutral, Truce, Ally.
role-single | Matches a specific relation
role-atleast | Matches a role of at least the given type. Order: Recruit, Normal, Moderator, Coleader, Admin.
role-atmost | Matches a role of at most the given type. Order: Recruit, Normal, Moderator, Coleader, Admin.
unknown | This is how any unknown selectors loaded from storage are treated, like if a plugin providing extra selectors didn't load. Unknown selectors never match anything.

title: PlaceholderAPI
excerpt: We hook into the plugin PlaceholderAPI to allow you to hook
bodyMarkdown:
# PlaceholderAPI

We hook into the plugin [PlaceholderAPI](https://www.spigotmc.org/resources/placeholderapi.6245/) to allow you to hook 
Factions info into other plugins.

## Internal Placeholders
Spots that you can use placeholders from other plugins in MxF Factions text:

* `/f show`
* scoreboards
* name tag prefix

## External Placeholders
These are placeholders you can use in other plugins (or the places listed above) that will hook into MxF Factions.

### Relations
The following can be used in plugins that support PlaceholderAPI relational placeholders.

Relational Placeholder | Description
--- | ---
%rel_factionsuuid_relation% | The relation between the 2 players
%rel_factionsuuid_relation_color% | Color of the relation between the 2 players

### Players
The rest of the placeholders should be usable by any plugin supporting placeholders.

Player Placeholder | Description
--- | ---
%factionsuuid_player_name% | The player's name

title: Player and Faction Leader Guide
excerpt: Server owners can rename, disable, or restrict commands. Use `/f help` for the live command list your rank is allowed to see.
bodyMarkdown:
# Player and Faction Leader Guide

Server owners can rename, disable, or restrict commands. Use `/f help` for the live command list your rank is allowed to see.

## Start a Faction

Create your own faction:

```text
/f create <name>
/f desc <description>
/f invite <player>
```

Or join an existing faction after receiving an invitation:

```text
/f join <faction>
```

Useful membership commands include `/f show`, `/f status`, `/f roster`, `/f alts`, `/f ban`, `/f unban`, and `/f showinvites`.

## Claim and Build

Claims are chunk-based. One chunk is 16 by 16 blocks horizontally and extends through the world height.

```text
/f claim
/f unclaim
/f map
/f seechunk
/f listclaims
```

Your server may use power, DTR, fixed limits, upgrades, costs, or season restrictions to control claim capacity. `/f show` and `/f power` or `/f dtr` show the active model.

Set travel points after securing land:

```text
/f sethome
/f home
/f setwarp <name>
/f warp
```

## Roles and Internal Permissions

Faction roles control membership authority. Common role tools are:

```text
/f promote <player>
/f demote <player>
/f coleader <player>
/f defaultrank <role>
/f perms
```

`/f perms` controls faction-owned actions such as building, containers, doors, home, warps, territory, economy, TNT, invitations, kicks, and promotions. It does not grant Bukkit staff permissions.

## Relations and Chat

```text
/f ally <faction>
/f truce <faction>
/f neutral <faction>
/f enemy <faction>
/f chat <mode>
```

Relation changes may require acceptance and can be limited by server configuration.

## Faction Storage and Economy

```text
/f money balance
/f money deposit <amount>
/f money withdraw <amount>
/f tnt info
/f tnt deposit <amount|all>
/f tnt withdraw <amount>
/f tnt fill <radius> <amount>
/f chest
/f vault
/f vaults
```

TNT fill is bounded by configured radius, block limits, territory rules, shield/timer restrictions, and faction TNT balance. Native faction vaults belong to the faction and survive normal restarts.

## Progression

```text
/f missions
/f missions info <id>
/f upgrades
/f upgrade info <id>
/f upgrade buy <id>
/f shop
/f pointshop
/f rewards
```

Mission objectives only progress from supported tracked events. Upgrade effects only apply after a successful purchase and must be supported by the active definition handler.

## Rankings and Seasons

```text
/f top
/f ptop
/f points
/f pointstop
/f season
/f season top
/f season history
/f hof
```

FTop is cached and does not perform a full value scan every time you open it. Season rewards may be preview-only until staff finalizes a season.

## Competitive Events

```text
/f koth
/f outpost
/f raidoutpost
/f raid
/f raid alerts
/f raid history
/f war
```

KOTH and Outposts use configured regions and eligibility rules. Raid Outposts add staged breach/entry/core objectives. The Live War Center combines active raid, alert, shield, TNT, claim, and member information.

## Defense

```text
/f shield
/f shield status
/f raid alerts settings
/f raid alerts ackall
```

Grace and SOTW are global staff-managed timers. Shields are faction windows and may have role, cooldown, duration, timezone, or change-delay rules.

## Analytics and Intelligence

```text
/f analytics
/f intelligence
/f intelligence guide
```

Analytics shows tracked faction activity. Intelligence produces a cached readiness report from analytics, raids, TNT, members, shields, upgrades, FTop and audit activity. It is an advisory score, not proof of cheating or a guaranteed raid outcome.

## Rules and Discipline

Players can view the rules and their faction's visible history where allowed:

```text
/f rules
/f rules list
/f rules history
/f rules sanctions
```

Private evidence, internal staff notes, and restricted investigation data are not shown to normal players.

## When a Command Fails

Check, in order:

1. The live usage in `/f help`.
2. Your Bukkit permission node.
3. Your faction role/action permission.
4. Whether the feature or command is disabled.
5. Season phase, Grace, SOTW, shield, raid, world, cooldown, and economy restrictions.
6. Whether an optional provider is installed.

title: Public Faction Profiles
excerpt: Public faction profiles are disabled by default in `mxf/web.yml`.
bodyMarkdown:
# Public Faction Profiles

Public faction profiles are disabled by default in `mxf/web.yml`.

When enabled, `GET /api/v1/public/factions/{tag}` returns safe public faction data such as tag, description, leader, member count, FTop summary, and Season summary.

Private fields remain hidden:

- balances unless explicitly added later
- TNT
- warps and home coordinates
- shield schedules
- active raid coordinates
- staff cases
- private member details

title: Release and Server QA Checklist
excerpt: Run this checklist on staging for every release candidate and meaningful configuration change.
bodyMarkdown:
# Release and Server QA Checklist

Run this checklist on staging for every release candidate and meaningful configuration change.

## Record the Environment

- MxF version and JAR SHA-256.
- Server software and Minecraft version.
- Java version.
- Dependency names/versions.
- Tester names and timestamps.
- Copy of the exact configuration preset/files under test.

## Fresh Install

- [ ] Start with an empty `plugins/Factions/` folder and valid license.
- [ ] Confirm every managed root, menu, message, feature and preset YAML generates.
- [ ] Confirm playable mission, upgrade, shop and reward definitions load.
- [ ] Confirm no startup `SEVERE`, unresolved placeholder, invalid material or invalid sound.
- [ ] Create a faction, invite/join, claim, set home/warp, save and restart.

## Migration

- [ ] Back up and hash the complete existing folder.
- [ ] Start the new JAR against a staging copy.
- [ ] Confirm custom YAML values are preserved and new defaults append safely.
- [ ] Confirm faction, player, board, economy, TNT, vault, progression, season, event, audit and staff data remains.
- [ ] Review every changed file before production deployment.

## Commands, Menus and Permissions

- [ ] Test normal member, officer/moderator, co-leader, leader, staff, admin and console.
- [ ] Test every documented command's success, bad syntax and permission-denial path.
- [ ] Open every enabled GUI and test empty, disabled, locked, previous, next, back, close, refresh, success and error states.
- [ ] Confirm admin commands are absent from normal-player help.
- [ ] Confirm no `null`, `N/A`, `MemorySection`, raw enum/internal ID or unresolved token is shown.

## Two-Faction Gameplay

- [ ] Relations and relation permissions.
- [ ] Claim, unclaim, overclaim and protection.
- [ ] Member role/action permissions.
- [ ] TNT, faction money, native vaults, missions and upgrades.
- [ ] Grace, SOTW, shields, raid claims, raid alerts and recaps.
- [ ] KOTH, Outpost and Raid Outpost capture/contesting.
- [ ] Exactly-once rewards and Season Point credit.

## Season Lifecycle

- [ ] Preseason, Grace, SOTW, Active, Final Freeze, EOTW, Ended and Archived.
- [ ] Restart in each phase and verify timestamps/policies.
- [ ] Frozen standings and snapshot stability.
- [ ] Hall of Fame creation on archive.
- [ ] Reward preview and guarded confirmation/execute behavior.
- [ ] No destructive reset unless deliberately enabled and separately tested.

## Reload, Restart and Failure

- [ ] Scoped reload each feature repeatedly and verify one task/listener remains.
- [ ] Restart during active timers/events and verify documented settlement behavior.
- [ ] Test missing and invalid YAML.
- [ ] Test corrupt JSON quarantine using disposable staging data.
- [ ] Test missing economy, PlaceholderAPI, WorldEdit and WorldGuard.
- [ ] Test unavailable worlds, offline players, insufficient funds and duplicate clicks.
- [ ] Verify clean `/f saveall` and normal shutdown.

## Performance

- [ ] Populate multiple factions, claims, definitions, events and leaderboard rows.
- [ ] Record tick time, memory, save duration and command/menu latency.
- [ ] Observe FTop refresh, analytics snapshots, event ticks and raid detection under load.
- [ ] Confirm bounded scans do not force-load unexpected chunks.

## Compatibility Matrix

At minimum, test representative supported targets with matching dependencies:

- Spigot 1.8.8 / Java 8.
- Paper 1.12.2 / Java 8.
- Paper 1.16.5 with its required Java runtime.
- A current supported Paper target with its required Java runtime.

Do not claim a version as verified based only on compilation or fail-closed license startup.

title: Raid Alerts
excerpt: MxF Raid Alerts add a persistent defense notification layer on top of the existing raid backend.
bodyMarkdown:
# Raid Alerts

MxF Raid Alerts add a persistent defense notification layer on top of the existing raid backend.

## Commands

- `/f raid alerts` opens the raid alert center or shows text history.
- `/f raid alerts history [page]` shows stored alerts.
- `/f raid alerts detail <id>` shows one alert.
- `/f raid alerts ack <id>` marks one alert as read.
- `/f raid alerts ackall` marks all faction alerts as read.
- `/f raid alerts settings` opens personal notification settings.
- `/f raid alerts test [severity]` creates a safe test alert for staff.
- `/f raid alerts inspect <raidId>` shows aggregate snapshot details for staff.
- `/f raid alerts reload` reloads raid-alert YAML.

## Persistence

- Alerts: `plugins/Factions/data/raid_alerts.json`
- Preferences: `plugins/Factions/data/raid_alert_preferences.json`

Corrupt files are quarantined and replaced with clean runtime data.

## Placeholders

- `%mxf_raid_alerts_unread%`
- `%mxf_raid_alerts_critical_unread%`
- `%mxf_raid_last_alert%`
- `%mxf_raid_last_alert_time%`
- `%mxf_raid_breach_confirmed%`
- `%mxf_raid_breach_count%`
- `%mxf_raid_explosions_recent%`
- `%mxf_faction_under_attack%`
- `%mxf_faction_active_raid_phase%`

## Notes

Raid alerts do not create a second raid system. They observe `RaidService`, explosion events, block break events, shield/timer blocks, and movement into active defended claims.

title: Raid Detection
excerpt: MxF raid detection is intentionally conservative.
bodyMarkdown:
# Raid Detection

MxF raid detection is intentionally conservative.

## Implemented Signals

- Raid started/refreshed/ended from `RaidService`.
- Raid claim created from `RaidService`.
- Hostile explosion observed in normal faction land.
- Claimed block destruction during an active raid.
- Confirmed breach when configured destroyed-block threshold is met.
- Valuable block and spawner destruction.
- Enemy entry into defended claims during active raid sessions after the configured minimum time inside.
- Shield/timer-blocked explosion attempts.

## Anti-Spam Controls

- `aggregation.group-same-type-seconds`
- `aggregation.maximum-alerts-per-faction-per-minute`
- `aggregation.duplicate-location-radius-blocks`
- `detection.breach.cooldown-seconds`

## Deferred

- Container interaction detection.
- Bossbar and particle notification delivery.
- Webhook/Discord delivery.
- More precise cannon attribution.
- Automatic staff discipline cases from repeated alert patterns.

title: Raid Notification Configuration
excerpt: Raid alert configuration lives in `plugins/Factions/mxf/raid-alerts.yml`.
bodyMarkdown:
# Raid Notification Configuration

Raid alert configuration lives in `plugins/Factions/mxf/raid-alerts.yml`.

## Important Sections

- `enabled` controls the whole system.
- `detection` controls signal types and thresholds.
- `aggregation` controls duplicate grouping and rate limits.
- `notifications` controls defender, attacker, and global delivery.
- `preferences` controls default player notification settings.
- `acknowledgment` controls read state behavior.
- `location-display` controls exact versus chunk-level coordinates.
- `retention` controls stored history.
- `audit` controls audit log emission.
- `severity` controls sound/channel/style per severity.
- `messages` controls player-facing text.

Menu presentation lives in `plugins/Factions/menus/raid-alerts.yml`; migrated installations may preserve the following section names:

- `menus.raid-alert-center`
- `menus.raid-alert-history`
- `menus.raid-alert-detail`
- `menus.raid-notification-settings`

Player preferences can be changed with `/f raid alerts settings`.

title: Raid Outpost Regions
excerpt: Each Raid Outpost definition uses four main cuboid regions:
bodyMarkdown:
# Raid Outpost Regions

Each Raid Outpost definition uses four main cuboid regions:

- `outer` - the full event area and default reset area.
- `breach` - the area where block destruction counts toward breach confirmation.
- `entry` - the area used to confirm attackers have entered the structure.
- `core` - the final capture area factions must control.

Regions are configured in `plugins/Factions/mxf/raid-outpost-definitions.yml`.

Example:

```yaml
raid-outposts:
  fortress:
world: "world"
regions:
  outer:
    minimum: { x: -8, y: 60, z: -8 }
    maximum: { x: 8, y: 78, z: 8 }
  breach:
    minimum: { x: -7, y: 60, z: -7 }
    maximum: { x: 7, y: 74, z: 7 }
  entry:
    minimum: { x: -5, y: 60, z: -5 }
    maximum: { x: 5, y: 72, z: 5 }
  core:
    minimum: { x: -2, y: 63, z: -2 }
    maximum: { x: 2, y: 66, z: 2 }
```

## WorldEdit Setup

If WorldEdit is installed, select a cuboid and run:

- `/f raidoutpost admin setouter fortress`
- `/f raidoutpost admin setbreach fortress`
- `/f raidoutpost admin setentry fortress`
- `/f raidoutpost admin setcore fortress`

The definition is saved immediately and validation runs on reload/startup.

## Validation

The loader normalizes min/max coordinates and skips invalid definitions. One bad definition does not disable the whole Raid Outpost system.

Recommended layout:

- Keep `core` inside `outer`.
- Keep `breach` inside or overlapping `outer`.
- Keep regions in the same world.
- Avoid very large reset regions until the block batch settings are tuned.

title: Raid Outpost Resets
excerpt: Raid Outposts support reset profiles in `plugins/Factions/mxf/raid-outposts.yml`.
bodyMarkdown:
# Raid Outpost Resets

Raid Outposts support reset profiles in `plugins/Factions/mxf/raid-outposts.yml`.

Implemented reset type:

- `BLOCK_SNAPSHOT`

Deferred reset types:

- `WORLDEDIT_SCHEMATIC`
- `COMMANDS`
- `CUSTOM`

## Block Snapshot Reset

Before an event starts, the plugin captures block type and legacy data from the configured reset region. After completion or staff reset, blocks are restored on the main thread in configurable batches.

Example:

```yaml
reset-profiles:
  safe:
type: BLOCK_SNAPSHOT
region: OUTER
blocks-per-tick: 500
teleport-players-out: true
remove-dropped-items: true
clear-projectiles: true
clear-explosives: true
restore-containers: false
fail-closed: true
```

## Safety Notes

- Bukkit world/block APIs are only used on the main thread.
- Large restores are chunked to avoid one-tick lag spikes.
- Dropped items inside the outer region can be cleared if configured.
- Players can be teleported out before reset if configured.
- Container inventories and tile entities are not restored in this pass.
- Active sessions are cancelled on shutdown, and rewards are not rerun after restart.

title: Raid Outpost Rewards
excerpt: Reward profiles live in `plugins/Factions/mxf/raid-outposts.yml`.
bodyMarkdown:
# Raid Outpost Rewards

Reward profiles live in `plugins/Factions/mxf/raid-outposts.yml`.

Example:

```yaml
reward-profiles:
  default:
entries:
  - type: SEASON_POINTS
    amount: 500
  - type: FACTION_MONEY
    amount: 25000
  - type: TNT
    amount: 10000
  - type: COMMANDS
    commands:
      - "broadcast {faction} captured {raid_outpost_name}!"
```

## Working Handlers

- `SEASON_POINTS` - adds points through the Season Points service.
- `FACTION_MONEY` - deposits through the existing economy integration.
- `TNT` - adds TNT to the faction bank.
- `COMMANDS` - runs configured console commands.

## Placeholders

- `{faction}`
- `{faction_id}`
- `{leader}`
- `{raid_outpost}`
- `{raid_outpost_name}`
- `{session_id}`
- `{season_points}`
- `{capture_time}`

## Duplicate Protection

The session reward state is persisted before external reward handlers run. This prevents reward duplication if a command or economy hook fails after some reward entries succeed.

If a faction is frozen, suspended, or payout-disqualified by competitive sanctions, it cannot receive Raid Outpost rewards.

title: Raid Outposts
excerpt: Raid Outposts are temporary raid objectives that combine breach detection, entry confirmation, KOTH-style core control, rewards, and safe reset handling.
bodyMarkdown:
# Raid Outposts

Raid Outposts are temporary raid objectives that combine breach detection, entry confirmation, KOTH-style core control, rewards, and safe reset handling.

The system is configured through YAML:

- `plugins/Factions/mxf/raid-outposts.yml` controls engine behavior, capture rules, breach rules, rewards, reset profiles, schedules, audit, messages, and history limits.
- `plugins/Factions/mxf/raid-outpost-definitions.yml` controls each outpost definition.
- `plugins/Factions/menus/raid-outposts.yml` controls the Raid Outpost GUI.
- `plugins/Factions/data/raid_outposts.json` stores runtime state, cooldowns, and history.

## Player Commands

- `/f raidoutpost` - list configured raid outposts.
- `/f raidoutposts` - alias for `/f raidoutpost`.
- `/f ro` - short alias.
- `/f raidop` - short alias.
- `/f raidoutpost list` - list configured raid outposts.
- `/f raidoutpost status [id]` - show the active state, phase, breach state, controller, progress, cooldown, and history summary.
- `/f raidoutpost menu` - open the configured GUI.
- `/f raidoutpost history [id] [page]` - view bounded completion history.

## Staff Commands

- `/f raidoutpost admin create <id>`
- `/f raidoutpost admin delete <id>`
- `/f raidoutpost admin setouter <id>`
- `/f raidoutpost admin setbreach <id>`
- `/f raidoutpost admin setentry <id>`
- `/f raidoutpost admin setcore <id>`
- `/f raidoutpost admin setteleport <id>`
- `/f raidoutpost admin start <id>`
- `/f raidoutpost admin stop <id>`
- `/f raidoutpost admin forcapture <id> <faction>`
- `/f raidoutpost admin reset <id>`
- `/f raidoutpost admin snapshot <id>`
- `/f raidoutpost admin setduration <id> <seconds>`
- `/f raidoutpost admin enable <id>`
- `/f raidoutpost admin disable <id>`
- `/f raidoutpost admin inspect <id>`
- `/f raidoutpost admin reload`
- `/f raidoutpost admin save`

## Lifecycle

1. The outpost starts manually or through a configured schedule.
2. A block snapshot is captured for the reset region.
3. Attackers breach the configured breach region.
4. Attackers enter the configured entry region.
5. One eligible faction controls the core region.
6. Progress reaches the configured capture duration.
7. Rewards are marked as executed before external reward handlers run.
8. The configured reset profile restores blocks safely on the main thread.
9. Cooldown and history are persisted.

## Season Policy

Scheduled Raid Outposts obey the Season Engine raid feature policy. Manual staff starts can bypass the blocked phase for controlled testing and administration.

## Restart Behavior

The safest default is used: active sessions are cancelled on shutdown and saved to history. Cooldowns and history persist. Rewards are not rerun after restart.

## Limitations

The current reset strategy restores block type and legacy data. Container inventories, tile entities, and schematic paste resets are intentionally deferred.

title: MxF Rewards
excerpt: Reward definitions are reusable actions that shops, staff tools, and future event systems can execute safely.
bodyMarkdown:
# MxF Rewards

Reward definitions are reusable actions that shops, staff tools, and future event systems can execute safely.

## Commands

- `/f rewards` lists reward definitions.
- `/f rewards validate` shows definition diagnostics.
- `/f rewards inspect <id>` inspects a reward or shop definition.
- `/f rewards give <player> <reward>` grants a configured reward manually.
- `/f rewards refund <purchaseId> [reason]` marks a purchase refunded.

## Reward Actions

- `money`: deposits personal Vault money.
- `faction-money`: deposits faction Vault money.
- `season-points`: adds Season Points to the faction.
- `xp-levels`: grants XP levels.
- `item`: gives an item using legacy-safe material resolution.
- `command`: runs configured console commands if enabled.
- `bundle`: chains other reward definitions.

## Safety Notes

Unknown action types are parsed but do not execute. Offline delivery queues and third-party token providers are deferred until a provider layer is added.

title: Rollback Conflicts
excerpt: Rollback preview compares current state with the recorded after-state.
bodyMarkdown:
# Rollback Conflicts

Rollback preview compares current state with the recorded after-state.

Strategies:

- `ABORT`: reject execution if current state changed.
- `DELTA`: apply the inverse delta when configured.
- `EXACT`: reserved for future exact restore flows.
- `MANUAL_REQUIRED`: show the event but do not execute automatically.

Default strategy is `ABORT`.

Conflict checks run again during execution. Tokens are one-time and expire.

title: Rollback Handlers
excerpt: Implemented handlers:
bodyMarkdown:
# Rollback Handlers

Implemented handlers:

- `TNT_BANK`: restores the faction TNT bank using recorded `before` and `after` values.
- `SEASON_POINTS`: restores Season Points through `SeasonPointsService.setPoints`.

Deferred handlers are disabled by default until their sources record complete before/after state:

- faction money
- upgrade level
- mission progress
- roster
- alts
- warps
- permissions
- shields
- sanctions and strikes
- claims

Rollback never calls arbitrary YAML-defined classes or commands.

title: Safe Rollback
excerpt: Rollback is preview-first. No mutation happens until staff confirms an actor-bound token.
bodyMarkdown:
# Safe Rollback

Rollback is preview-first. No mutation happens until staff confirms an actor-bound token.

Commands:

- `/f rollback preview <eventId>`
- `/f rollback select <eventId>`
- `/f rollback deselect <eventId>`
- `/f rollback selection`
- `/f rollback clear`
- `/f rollback preview selected`
- `/f rollback confirm <token> <reason>`
- `/f rollback history`
- `/f rollback detail <rollbackId>`
- `/f rollback undo preview <rollbackId>`
- `/f rollback undo confirm <token> <reason>`
- `/f rollback menu`

Rollback records are permanent evidence and are not deleted when undone.

title: Season Engine
excerpt: The MxF Season Engine coordinates existing season systems without replacing their storage.
bodyMarkdown:
# Season Engine

The MxF Season Engine coordinates existing season systems without replacing their storage.

It controls the current season identity, lifecycle phase, phase timestamps, transition history, final freeze state, reward preview readiness, and safe reward execution state.

The engine is non-destructive by default. It does not reset factions, claims, balances, upgrades, missions, Season Points, or worlds.

Main commands:
- `/f season`
- `/f season info`
- `/f season phase`
- `/f season transition <phase> [force confirm] [reason]`
- `/f season freeze [status|rebuild]`
- `/f season snapshot`
- `/f season rewards status|preview|confirm|execute`
- `/f season admin inspect`
- `/f season start <id> <display name> confirm`
- `/f season end confirm`
- `/f season archive confirm`

State is stored in `plugins/Factions/data/season_engine.json`.

Beta safety defaults require explicit confirmation for start/end/archive and keep reward execution disabled while `seasonRewards.previewOnly` or `seasons.betaSafety.rewardExecutionEnabled` blocks it.

title: Season Phases
excerpt: Valid phase order:
bodyMarkdown:
# Season Phases

Valid phase order:

`OFF_SEASON -> PRESEASON -> GRACE -> SOTW -> ACTIVE -> FINAL_FREEZE -> EOTW -> ENDED -> ARCHIVED`

Each phase is configurable in `plugins/Factions/mxf/seasons.yml`.

Phase policy can block:
- claims and unclaims
- TNT actions
- raids and raid claims
- KOTH starts
- outpost capture/reward ticks
- mission progress
- Season Points mutation
- upgrade purchases
- roster and alt changes
- shield and warp changes
- faction disbanding

Forced transitions require `factions.season.force` and `confirm` when beta safety requires confirmation.

title: MxF Season Points
excerpt: Season Points are a persistent, non-negative competitive score stored per normal faction ID.
bodyMarkdown:
# MxF Season Points

Season Points are a persistent, non-negative competitive score stored per normal faction ID.

## Commands

| Command | Permission | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `/f points` | `factions.points` | Shows your faction's Season Points. |
| `/f points <faction>` | `factions.points.others` | Shows another faction's Season Points. |
| `/f pointstop [page]` | `factions.pointstop` | Shows the cached Season Points leaderboard. |
| `/f points add <faction> <amount> [reason]` | `factions.points.admin`, `factions.points.admin.add` | Adds points. |
| `/f points remove <faction> <amount> [reason]` | `factions.points.admin`, `factions.points.admin.remove` | Removes points without allowing negative totals. |
| `/f points set <faction> <amount> [reason]` | `factions.points.admin`, `factions.points.admin.set` | Sets a faction's point total. |
| `/f points refresh` | `factions.points.admin`, `factions.points.admin.refresh` | Rebuilds the cached leaderboard. |

Aliases include `/f seasonpoints`, `/f spoints`, `/f seasonpointstop`, `/f spointstop`, and `/f stop`.

## Config

Generated file:

`plugins/Factions/mxf/season-points.yml`

Important sections:

- `enabled`
- `number-format`
- `default-reason`
- `leaderboard.page-size`
- `leaderboard.refresh-interval-seconds`
- `leaderboard.show-zero-point-factions`
- `audit.log-changes`
- `audit.log-rejected-changes`
- `messages`

## Storage

Data file:

`plugins/Factions/data/season_points.json`

Storage is keyed by FactionsUUID faction ID, so faction renames keep points intact. Disbanded factions are cleaned up through the Season Points cleanup listener.

## Earning Hooks

The first safe earning hook is the existing MxF outpost interval reward. If an outpost definition has a positive `season-points` reward, that value is added through `SeasonPointsService` when the interval reward is issued.

## PlaceholderAPI

The bundled `mxf` expansion supports:

- `%mxf_faction_season_points%`
- `%mxf_faction_season_points_formatted%`
- `%mxf_faction_season_rank%`
- `%mxf_season_points_top_1_name%`
- `%mxf_season_points_top_1_points%`
- `%mxf_season_points_top_1_points_formatted%`

Top placeholders support ranks 1 through 10.

title: Season Rewards
excerpt: Season rewards remain preview-first.
bodyMarkdown:
# Season Rewards

Season rewards remain preview-first.

Reward tiers are still configured in `plugins/Factions/mxf/season-rewards.yml`.

The Season Engine adds:
- `/f season rewards status`
- `/f season rewards confirm`
- `/f season rewards execute`

By default, rewards do not execute because:
- `season-rewards.yml` uses `preview-only: true`
- `seasons.yml` uses `beta-safety.reward-execution-enabled: false`

Both must be intentionally changed before command execution can run.

Reward previews use frozen final standings when available.

title: Season Snapshots
excerpt: Season snapshots are generated from cached services where possible.
bodyMarkdown:
# Season Snapshots

Season snapshots are generated from cached services where possible.

Snapshot data includes:
- faction id and tag
- FTop value
- analytics score
- intelligence score
- TNT activity
- raid count
- upgrade count
- member count
- claim count

Snapshots are stored by the existing `SeasonService` in `data/seasons.json`.

The Season Engine also stores final freeze metadata in `data/season_engine.json` so reward preview standings remain stable after final freeze.

title: MxF Shop
excerpt: The MxF shop is a configurable reward shop backed by YAML definitions and purchase history.
bodyMarkdown:
# MxF Shop

The MxF shop is a configurable reward shop backed by YAML definitions and purchase history.

## Commands

- `/f shop` opens the default shop GUI when menus are enabled.
- `/f shop menu [shop]` opens a specific shop GUI.
- `/f shop list [shop] [page]` shows text fallback entries.
- `/f shop buy [shop] <item> confirm` purchases an item.
- `/f shop history [page]` shows relevant purchase history.
- `/f shop admin reload` reloads reward economy YAML.
- `/f shop admin validate` shows definition diagnostics.
- `/f shop admin inspect <id>` inspects a shop or reward definition.
- `/f shop admin refund <purchaseId> [reason]` marks a purchase refunded and restores reversible currencies.

## YAML

- `mxf/rewards.yml` controls global reward economy behavior.
- `mxf/shop-definitions.yml` defines shops and items.
- `mxf/reward-definitions.yml` defines reward actions.
- `data/purchase-history.json` stores purchase records.

## Supported Prices

- `money`
- `faction-money`
- `season-points`
- `xp-levels`
- `item`

Unsupported currencies fail closed until a provider is registered.

## Beta Default Catalog

The generated default catalog now includes testable personal and faction examples:

- Personal money pack paid with XP levels.
- Faction season bundle paid with Season Points.
- Utility supply bundle paid with personal money.
- Limited faction booster paid with faction money and Season Points.

Defaults use existing reward providers and preserve server-owner edits.

title: Staff and Administration Guide
excerpt: Staff tooling is powerful and should be granted by responsibility instead of giving every moderator `factions.*`.
bodyMarkdown:
# Staff and Administration Guide

Staff tooling is powerful and should be granted by responsibility instead of giving every moderator `factions.*`.

## Daily Operations

```text
/f version
/f compatibility report
/f config status
/f config validate
/f saveall
/f logs
```

Use scoped reloads for YAML changes. Restart for JARs, dependencies, license changes, and major integration changes.

## Permission Roles

Recommended separation:

| Staff role | Typical responsibility |
| --- | --- |
| Helper | Read-only player/faction information and basic support. |
| Moderator | Bypass/chat spy, member moderation, rule cases, read-only investigations. |
| Senior staff | Event control, evidence, sanctions, rollback preview. |
| Administrator | Configuration, definitions, season control, reward execution, rollback confirmation. |
| Console/owner | License, JAR/dependencies, backups, emergency recovery. |

Review Permission Nodes and test each group with a real non-operator account.

## Seasons

The Season Engine is non-destructive unless separately configured otherwise.

```text
/f season start <id> <display name> confirm
/f season info
/f season phase
/f season transition <phase> [force confirm] [reason]
/f season snapshot
/f season freeze status
/f season top
/f season rewards status
/f season rewards preview
/f season end confirm
/f season archive confirm
```

Keep reward execution disabled while testing. Archive records final data and Hall of Fame entries; it does not reset factions, claims, money, TNT, missions, or upgrades.

## KOTH, Outposts, and Raid Outposts

Configure regions in staging before enabling schedules. Use the dedicated guides:

- KOTH
- Outposts
- Raid Outposts
- Raid Outpost Regions
- Raid Outpost Rewards
- Raid Outpost Resets

Verify eligibility, contesting, reward timing, duplicate resistance, shutdown, restart, and unloaded-world behavior.

## Raid Control and Alerts

```text
/f raid control
/f raid info <faction>
/f raid alerts inspect <raidId>
/f raid alerts test <severity>
/f raid history
/f raid recap <raidId>
```

Raid alerts aggregate tracked signals; they do not prove cheating. Tune thresholds against real server traffic before escalating automatically.

## Competitive Rules and Sanctions

Rule definitions live in `mxf/rule-definitions.yml`; engine safeguards live in `mxf/competitive-rules.yml`.

Staff can issue warnings/strikes, add evidence references and private notes, change appeal state, apply supported penalties, inspect sanctions, and undo reversible penalties. Exact syntax is under the Competitive Rules section of Commands.

Safe policy:

1. Automatic detections create alerts/cases first.
2. A human reviews evidence.
3. Staff records a reason and evidence reference.
4. Reversible penalties are preferred.
5. Season Point deductions and payout disqualification are verified against frozen standings.
6. Private notes remain staff-only.
7. Every reversal keeps its audit record.

## Investigations

```text
/f investigate recent [category] [page]
/f investigate faction <faction> [page]
/f investigate player <player> [page]
/f investigate event <eventId>
/f investigate related <eventId>
/f investigate transactions <faction>
/f investigate search <text>
/f investigate export faction <faction>
```

Investigations index existing audit data. Exports can contain sensitive player/faction activity and should remain private.

## Safe Rollback

Rollback is preview-first:

```text
/f rollback preview <eventId>
/f rollback select <eventId>
/f rollback preview selected
/f rollback confirm <token> <reason>
/f rollback history
/f rollback undo preview <rollbackId>
/f rollback undo confirm <token> <reason>
```

Confirmation tokens are actor-bound and time-limited. Review conflicts, current balances/state, and the audit trail before confirming. Rollback records remain even after undo.

## Configuration and Presets

```text
/f config status
/f config validate [scope]
/f config reload <scope>
/f preset preview <preset>
/f preset diff <preset>
/f preset apply <preset> confirm
/f preset rollback <backup> confirm
```

Presets edit explicit YAML paths and create backups. They do not wipe runtime JSON.

## Staff Shift Checklist

- [ ] Read startup/reload warnings.
- [ ] Check active season phase, Grace, SOTW and shield policy.
- [ ] Check active raids and critical alerts.
- [ ] Check scheduled KOTH/Outpost/Raid Outpost definitions.
- [ ] Review open competitive cases and unresolved investigations.
- [ ] Confirm autosave is running and the latest external backup succeeded.
- [ ] Record exact event IDs, case IDs, and rollback IDs in staff notes.

title: Troubleshooting
excerpt: Start with the first Factions-related warning or exception, not the last shutdown symptom.
bodyMarkdown:
# Troubleshooting

Start with the first Factions-related warning or exception, not the last shutdown symptom.

## Collect This Information

- MxF Factions version and JAR SHA-256.
- Server software and Minecraft version.
- Java version.
- Installed dependency names and versions.
- Full startup block or complete stack trace.
- Exact command/action and reproduction steps.
- Relevant YAML with secrets removed.
- Whether the issue survives a clean restart.

## Startup Problems

| Symptom | Likely cause | Action |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Plugin disables before services load | Missing/invalid license, wrong product/IP/HWID, activation or API denial. | Read the safe license result, correct the portal/key, restart. Do not post the key. |
| `UnsupportedClassVersionError` | Java runtime cannot load the JAR or a dependency. | Verify Java and the named class/JAR. MxF targets major version 52. |
| `InvalidPluginException` | Wrong/corrupt/stale JAR or missing shaded class. | Compare hash, remove duplicate JARs, redeploy the release artifact. |
| Missing dependency class | Incompatible optional plugin or accidentally unshaded library. | Remove/replace the named dependency with a compatible build and restart. |
| YAML parse exception | Invalid indentation, quote, list or scalar/object type. | Restore the last valid YAML, validate, then reapply the change carefully. |
| Shutdown exception after failed enable | Startup stopped before a service initialized. | Fix the original enable exception first; update if the current build lacks guarded shutdown. |

## Commands and Permissions

| Symptom | Check |
| --- | --- |
| Command missing | `features.yml`, `commands.yml`, conditional integration mode, startup command registration. |
| Permission denied | Bukkit node, parent inheritance, world/context, faction role/action permission. |
| Admin command shown to players | Permission group inheritance and `/f help` filtering. |
| Wrong usage/alias | Live `/f help`, command entry in `commands.yml`, alias conflicts with other plugins. |
| Command works from console but not player | Player context, faction requirement, role/action permission, world/season restriction. |

## Menus and Messages

| Symptom | Action |
| --- | --- |
| Menu does not open | Confirm menus enabled, player sender, permission, valid size/material, feature enabled. |
| Blank paper/item | Check the feature definition and menu placeholder source; run config validation. |
| `null`, `N/A`, `MemorySection`, raw enum or `{value}` | A YAML section/type or unresolved placeholder is wrong. Fix the owning message/menu/definition path. |
| Click does nothing | Confirm action type, permission, enabled state, safe command allowlist and dependency state. |
| Wrong sound/material on 1.8.8 | Add the documented modern-to-legacy fallback. |
| Edit not visible | Reload the owning scope and reopen the inventory. |

## Gameplay Systems

| Symptom | Check |
| --- | --- |
| Claims denied | Power/DTR/capacity, cost, world, phase, roster, Grace/SOTW, sanction and role permission. |
| TNT fill misses containers | Radius, bounded scan cap, territory rule, unloaded chunks, dispenser/dropper toggles, balance and timer restrictions. |
| FTop empty | Value definitions, excluded worlds, cached refresh, claim data and chunk budget. |
| PTop unavailable | No safe personal contribution source is active; the command does not fabricate values. |
| Mission not progressing | Objective type, filters, assignment state, event hook and source ID. |
| Upgrade not applying | Definition/level purchase, effect handler and consuming subsystem support. |
| Shop/point shop empty | Enabled shop and reward definitions, category/menu filters and valid price provider. |
| KOTH/Outpost not capturing | World/region, eligibility, faction count, contest rules, vanished/dead/spectator filtering. |
| Event reward delayed | First-reward delay, minimum hold, interval, cooldown and duplicate-protection state. |
| Raid alert missing | Detection toggle, protected/ignored source, aggregation window, preference and acknowledgment state. |
| Season action blocked | Phase order, confirmation token, freeze, beta safety and staff permission. |
| Intelligence seems low | Allow analytics snapshots/activity to accumulate; inspect configured weights and missing data. |

## Reload and Restart

After repeated reloads, verify there is still one task per scheduler-owned service and one listener registration. If rewards, announcements, or captures run twice, stop the server and restore the last known-good configuration/build before further testing.

Active KOTH or Raid Outpost sessions may intentionally settle/cancel on shutdown rather than resume, depending on current safety policy. Review the feature guide before treating this as data loss.

## Reporting a Bug

Use this format:

```text
MxF version:
JAR SHA-256:
Server software/version:
Java version:
Dependencies:
Action performed:
Expected result:
Actual result:
Reproduction steps:
Console error:
Relevant config (secrets removed):
Frequency:
Data affected:
```

title: MxF Factions Visual Style Guide
excerpt: MxF Factions should feel like one complete competitive game, not a bundle of unrelated plugin screens. Every new MxF feature must use the shared visual language in this guide.
bodyMarkdown:
# MxF Factions Visual Style Guide

## Purpose

MxF Factions should feel like one complete competitive game, not a bundle of unrelated plugin screens. Every new MxF feature must use the shared visual language in this guide.

## Core Palette

Use colors by meaning:

- Gold `&6`: titles, rankings, premium values, progress, achievements.
- Aqua `&b`: faction names, interactive information, clickable stats.
- Green `&a`: success, enabled states, completed actions, purchases.
- Red `&c`: errors, unavailable actions, disabled states, locked content.
- Orange/yellow `&e`: warnings, timers, temporary or contested states.
- Gray `&7`: labels, descriptions, secondary text.
- Dark gray `&8`: separators, borders, inactive chrome.
- White `&f`: readable values and player names.

Do not randomly color text. Color communicates state and hierarchy.

## Symbols

Symbols are configured in `mxf/visuals.yml -> symbols`. Defaults are ASCII-safe for Minecraft 1.8.8.

Standard keys include:

- `factions`, `combat`, `shields`, `economy`, `tokens`, `season-points`
- `koth`, `outposts`, `statistics`, `history`, `missions`, `rewards`
- `storage`, `raid`, `tnt`, `upgrades`, `rankings`, `settings`
- `members`, `territory`, `timers`, `locked`, `completed`, `disabled`, `warning`, `info`

Use `{icon_<key>}` placeholders in menu lore/messages instead of hardcoding symbols.

## Menu Layout

All MxF inventory menus must keep navigation consistent:

- Back: bottom-left, slot `-9`.
- Close: bottom-center, slot `-5`.
- Refresh: bottom row near close, slot `-4`.
- Previous page: bottom-left, slot `-9` when there is no back action.
- Current page: bottom-center, slot `-5` when there is no close action conflict.
- Next page: bottom-right, slot `-1`.
- Header/info items should not block expected action slots.

Menus should be symmetrical, grouped by purpose, and readable at a glance.

## Theme Presets

`menus/shared.yml -> theme.active` selects the global preset.

Built-in presets:

- `aaa-dark`: default premium shell.
- `default`, `dark`, `gold`, `red`, `purple`, `minimal`: compatibility presets.
- `faction`, `raid`, `season`, `economy`, `administration`, `rewards`, `events`, `battle-pass`: category border presets for specialized menus.

Feature menus may override materials/lore/items, but navigation and state meanings should remain consistent.

## Lore Format

Use this order:

1. Short description.
2. Blank separator line only when it improves scanning.
3. Status or progress.
4. Requirements.
5. Rewards or impact.
6. Click action.

Keep lore short. Avoid paragraphs, developer terms, and raw config names.

## Progress Bars

Use the shared progress bar in `mxf/visuals.yml -> progress-bar`.

Default format is ASCII-safe:

`&8[&6||||||&8||||||&8] &760%`

Do not mix unrelated progress bar styles.

## Messages

Messages should be concise and player-facing.

Use:

- `{visual_separator}` for framed blocks.
- Gold for titles.
- Gray labels and white values.
- Green success, red failure, yellow warnings/timers.

Avoid raw debug output, stack-like wording, inconsistent capitalization, and developer jargon.

## Placeholders

Use formatted placeholders whenever possible:

- `{season_points_formatted}`
- `{ftop_value}`
- `{tnt}`
- `{visual_separator}`
- `{icon_koth}`
- `{icon_outposts}`

Future numeric placeholders should be formatted through `MxFVisuals.formatNumber`.

## Performance Rules

- Do not rebuild menus every tick.
- Do not run scans inside placeholder rendering.
- Do not spam particles, sounds, titles, or holograms.
- Cache expensive views and read from existing services.
- Keep visual effects meaningful and low-frequency.

## Future Feature Rule

Before adding a new MxF feature, define:

- menu category/theme
- primary icon
- state colors
- progress format, if applicable
- sound feedback
- placeholder output format
- fallback behavior for 1.8.8

title: Web Authentication
excerpt: The production dashboard uses Minecraft-first, code-first onboarding.
bodyMarkdown:
# Web Authentication

The production dashboard uses Minecraft-first, code-first onboarding.

1. An eligible faction leader or co-leader runs `/f panel link` in Minecraft.
2. The production backend synchronizes current faction state and returns a short-lived, single-use `MXF-...` code.
3. Clicking the code on Minecraft 1.8.8 places it in the chat input so it can be copied with `Ctrl+A`, `Ctrl+C`.
4. The player enters the code on the website.
5. The backend verifies the code, installation, Minecraft UUID, current faction membership, and current role before showing account creation.
6. A first-time player creates their display name, recovery email, and password only after Minecraft proof succeeds.
7. A returning player signs in; the retained code is then exchanged for faction access.

Public account registration without an in-game code is disabled. A code is stored only as a SHA-256 hash, expires after the configured TTL, is consumed transactionally, and cannot be reused. Expired, used, revoked, and invalid codes have distinct API states.

Email verification is recommended for account recovery but does not replace Minecraft proof of faction access. Discord sign-in can be added later as another account authentication method without changing the faction-link trust model.

Production commands:

- `/f panel link`
- `/f panel status`
- `/f panel revokeall`
- `/f panel admin status`
- `/f panel admin reload`

The legacy embedded web integration may retain `/f web link <website-code>` when the production backend bridge is disabled.

title: MxF Web Integration
excerpt: MxF web integration uses a three-layer model: plugin API, website server proxy, and browser dashboard.
bodyMarkdown:
# MxF Web Integration

MxF web integration uses a three-layer model: plugin API, website server proxy, and browser dashboard.

The plugin API is disabled by default in `plugins/Factions/mxf/web.yml`. When enabled, it binds to the configured address and port, validates signed requests, and returns detached DTOs only.

Important files:

- `mxf/web.yml`
- `mxf/web-dashboard.yml`
- `mxf/web-permissions.yml`
- `mxf/web-theme.yml`
- `data/web_links.json`
- `data/web_sessions.json`
- `data/web_pending_actions.json`

The browser must never read plugin JSON files or submit authoritative faction state. Every sensitive request is resolved by the plugin from the linked Minecraft UUID and current faction role.

## Beta Dashboard Polish

The website now has a richer local default shell:

- Locked navigation before account linking.
- Three-step account linking page.
- Copyable `/f web link <code>` command.
- Dashboard hero, stat cards, right sidebar, activity, member, mission, shop, and event widgets.
- Configurable branding/theme hooks from the plugin dashboard configuration endpoint.
- Clear unlinked, linked-without-faction, API-offline, and empty states.

Build command:

```text
node scripts/build.mjs
```

## Production Code-First Linking

With `mxf/web-backend.yml` enabled, `/f panel link` requests a faction-bound code from the production backend. The browser previews the verified faction and Minecraft identity before allowing credentials to be created. Direct browser registration is intentionally disabled.

On Minecraft 1.8.8, the generated code is a clickable `suggest_command` chat component. Clicking places the code in the input box; the player can then copy it with `Ctrl+A`, `Ctrl+C`. Newer clipboard-only chat actions are not used because they do not exist in the 1.8 protocol.

`mxf/web-permissions.yml` maps web access levels to module actions.

title: Web Security
excerpt: Security controls implemented in the foundation:
bodyMarkdown:
# Web Security

Security controls implemented in the foundation:

- API key and secret.
- HMAC SHA-256 signatures over method, path, timestamp, nonce, and body.
- Timestamp expiry.
- Nonce replay protection.
- Read/write rate limits.
- CORS allowlist.
- Session expiry and revocation.
- Link-code expiry and one-time use.
- Privacy toggles for player IPs and exact coordinates.
- Faction-visible audit filtering.

The website may display and request actions, but Java remains authoritative. Browser-supplied faction ids, roles, permissions, balances, prices, rewards, and staff status are never trusted.
